## PAGE 00414: Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram

- page_id: `191926572`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926572/Creating+OV-5a+Operational+Activity+Model+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-14T09:48:28.828+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint > OV-5 Operational Activity Model
- labels: ['create-ov-5a-operational-activity-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Activity Model represents Operational Activity hierarchies. It must be created first.

To create an OV-5a diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies

1. Create Operational Activities.
2. Create Operational Performers or reuse them from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] (recommended) .
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Operational Performers and Operational Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Operational Activity Model represents Operational Activity hierarchies. It must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an OV-5a diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activities.</li><li>Create Operational Performers or reuse them from <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (recommended) .</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Operational Performers and Operational Activities.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926573 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00415: Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram

- page_id: `191926573`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926573/Creating+OV-5b+Operational+Activity+Flow+Model+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-14T09:48:46.527+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint > OV-5 Operational Activity Model
- labels: ['create-ov-5b-operational-activity-flow-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Operational Activity Flow Model represents Operational Activity flows. Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] must be created first.

To create an OV-5b diagram for Operational Activity flows

1. Create Operational Activity Actions or just drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect Operational Activity Actions wit h Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.
3. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Operational Activity Flow Model represents Operational Activity flows. Before creating this diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating OV-5a Operational Activity Model diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Activity Model diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an OV-5b diagram for Operational Activity flows</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activity Actions or just drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect Operational Activity Actions wit<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">h Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.</span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926578 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00416: Creating OV-6a table

- page_id: `191926578`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926578/Creating+OV-6a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T07:31:56.337+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Operational viewpoint > OV-6a Operational Rules Model
- labels: ['add-operational-constraint-in-ov-6a', 'create-ov-6a-operational-rules-model']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an OV-6a table, do one of the following

- Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the Add New button. The new row is added in table. Fill in three columns: 
- Applies To . Select the cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] . In the Select Subject Of Operational Constraint dialog, select constrained Operational Elements (Operational Performer, Operational Activity, Information Element, Operational Exchange). 
- **Rule****Specification**. Select the cell and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. In the **Specification** dialog, select the language and specify body with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL). 
- **Rule Kind**. Click the cell and from drop-down list select the rule kind.
- Add an existing Operational Constraints by clicking the Add Existing button. In Select Operational Constraint dialog, select Operational Constraints. The filled row is added in table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an OV-6a table, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the <strong>Add New </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> button. The new row is added in table. Fill in three columns:<br />-  </span><strong>Applies To</strong>. Select the cell and click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. In the <strong>Select Subject Of Operational Constraint</strong> dialog, select constrained Operational Elements (Operational Performer, Operational Activity, Information Element, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Operational Exchange).<br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">- <strong style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Rule </strong><strong>Specification</strong>. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select the cell</span> and click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>. In the <strong>Specification</strong> dialog, select the language and specify body with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).<br />- <strong>Rule Kind</strong>. Click the cell and from drop-down list select the rule kind.<br /></span></span></li><li>Add an existing Operational Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. In <strong>Select Operational Constraint</strong> dialog, select Operational Constraints.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> The filled row is added in table. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><br /><span> </span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /> </span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927274 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00417: Creating P1 Resource Types diagram

- page_id: `191927274`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927274/Creating+P1+Resource+Types+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-20T07:35:34.269+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-resource-types']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P1 Resource Types diagram

1. Create Resources.
2. Connect Resources using Resource Associations.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Associations.

The P1 Resource Types diagram also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Competences provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P2 Resource Internal Structure' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P4 Resource Functions' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view as soon as the P1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a P1 Resource Types diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resources.</li><li>Connect Resources using Resource Associations.</li><li>Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Associations.</li></ol><p>The P1 Resource Types diagram also allows you to show the Capabilities and Resources mapping the provided and required services and Competences provided by the Resources. You can model each Resource internal structure using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="P2 Resource Internal Structure" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45fbb702-bd2a-4e42-9ae9-b6702a7e6257"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="P4 Resource Functions" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the P1 has been completed.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927279 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00418: Creating P1 Service Provision matrix

- page_id: `191927279`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927279/Creating+P1+Service+Provision+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-20T07:39:34.674+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-service-provision-matrix', 'build-p1-service-provision-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The rows in a P1 Service Provision matrix represent Service Interfaces and the columns represent Resources.

To create a P1 Service Provision matrix

1. Specify the Rows scope (Service Interfaces).
2. Specify the Columns scope (Resources).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

The cells in the matrix represent a Resource Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The rows in a P1 Service Provision matrix represent Service Interfaces and the columns represent Resources.</p><p>To create a P1 Service Provision matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Rows scope</strong> (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Columns scope</strong> (Resources).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>The cells in the matrix represent a Resource Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927276 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00419: Creating P1 Systems to Systems Matrix

- page_id: `191927276`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927276/Creating+P1+Systems+to+Systems+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:57.070+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-systems-to-systems-matrix', 'build-p1-systems-to-systems-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Systems to Systems Matrix (P1)****shows how resources interact with each otherdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2'].A P1 Systems to Systems Matrix is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.

Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. The P1 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.

To create a P1 Systems to Systems matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (System Resources).
2. Specify the Column Scope (System Resources).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Systems to Systems Matrix (P1)<strong> </strong><span>shows how resources interact with each other </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span>A P1 Systems to Systems Matrix is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.</p><p>Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. The P1 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a P1 Systems to Systems matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927277 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00420: Creating P1 Technology Forecast table

- page_id: `191927277`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927277/Creating+P1+Technology+Forecast+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-11-19T12:58:34.875+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-p1-technology-forecast-table']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658799737

1658799749

1658799738

To create a P1 Technology Forecast table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658799736

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927283 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00421: Creating P2 Structure diagram

- page_id: `191927283`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927283/Creating+P2+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:57.139+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P2 Resource Structure
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### topCreating a diagram

To create a P2 Resource Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the P2 package and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**P2 Resource Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **P2****Resource Structure,**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Resource Artifact element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create a Resource Artifact in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Resource Artifact and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P2 Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To create a Resource Artifact in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the Resource Structure package, and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Resource Artifact and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='P2_drag_resource_artifact.png']

Resource Artifact

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Resource Artifact (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2024xR2'].

##### Connecting the Resource Artifact

When you have Resource Artifact created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P2 Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P2 Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Resource Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Aggregation End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Association** and choose **Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element, which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='resources.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P2 Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='resources.str.set_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P2 Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose to use this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose to use this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose to use this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2'] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2024xR2']** dialog, select **Association.** If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the **Resource Association** also.
4. Click OK .

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P2 Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024xR2'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='resources.str.compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P2 Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024xR2'] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2'] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2024xR2'] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='resources.str.display_related_nested.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P2 Structure diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f96904b-939d-4d80-9234-c113de640666"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating a diagram</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a P2 Resource Structure diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment Tree, select the P2 package and do one of the following:</span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> P2 Resource <span>Structure</span></strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="205" ac:width="307"><ri:attachment ri:filename="P2_new_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P2 Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>P2 </strong><strong>Resource <span>Structure,</span> </strong>and select it.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating an element</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Resource Artifact element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.<br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a Resource Artifact in a diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the diagram palette, click the Resource Artifact and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="265" ac:width="506"><ri:attachment ri:filename="P2_new_resource_artifact.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P2 Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P2 Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a Resource Artifact in a Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment tree, right-click the Resource Structure package, and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong>. Search for the Resource Artifact and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="310" ac:width="314"><ri:attachment ri:filename="P2_new_resource_artifact_palette.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P2 Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="266" ac:width="777"><ri:attachment ri:filename="P2_drag_resource_artifact.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P2 Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7d01530-058c-4324-a12b-1096609019f2"><ac:rich-text-body>The structure of each <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Resource Artifact</span> can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="P2 Resource Internal Structure" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Resource Artifact</span> (or other appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b78b18b-abe1-4fe3-a29c-3e9af9c1ac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connecting the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Resource Artifact</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When you have Resource Artifact created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.</span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P2 Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>To connect the elements with the Composition relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the Smart Manipulator, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> which appears after you select the element, choose</span> Directed Composition.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0780fc43-c960-4eb5-907e-da21eeb36ba3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="P2_new_direct_composition.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P2 Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="269" ac:width="906"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources_structure_draw_composition.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P2 Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P2 Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Resource Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.</p><p><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources.str.information_in_containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P2 Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Aggregation End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the diagram palette, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">click the black arrow near the <strong>Association</strong> and choose <strong>Aggregation</strong></span>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources_aggregation_palette.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P2 Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the element, which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity</h3><p>To change the role name</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Name</strong>. The default name will be displayed.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="198" ac:width="373"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources.str.change_role_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P2 Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. </p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P2 Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>To specify multiplicity</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.</li><li>At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="672" ac:width="368"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources.str.set_multiplicity.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P2 Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P2 Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><h3>Displaying created structure</h3><p>If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:</p><ul><li>Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose to use this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.</li><li>Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose to use this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.</li><li>Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose to use this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Association.</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abe3734a-44b5-4d45-8a04-40ea23304a35"><ac:rich-text-body>If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the <strong>Resource Association</strong> also.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>.</li></ol><p ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="891" ac:width="740"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources.str.display_related.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P2 Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Attribute Compartment Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Select the element ant click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources.str.compartments.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P2 Structure diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927305 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00422: Creating P2i diagram

- page_id: `191927305`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927305/Creating+P2i+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-29T09:11:24.050+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P2 Resource Internal Structure
- labels: ['create-p2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P2i diagram

1. Create Resources Roles or drag Resources from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.
2. Specify the types of the Resource Roles.
3. Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors or Resource Interfaces.
4. Create Resource Ports.
5. Specify the types for the Resource Ports.

A P2i diagram allows to show the provided and required services by the particular Resource Roles.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To create a P2i diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create Resources Roles or drag Resources from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Specify the types of the Resource Roles.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors or Resource Interfaces.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create Resource Ports.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Specify the types for the Resource Ports.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">A P2i diagram allows to show the provided and required services by the particular Resource Roles.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927312 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00423: Creating P3 table

- page_id: `191927312`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927312/Creating+P3+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-20T08:22:30.202+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P3 Resource Connectivity
- labels: ['create-p3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P3 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P3 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] Resource Exchangesto fill in a P3 table.

newTo add new Resource Exchange

1. In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

existingTo add an existing Resource Exchange

1. In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Resource Exchanges by clicking + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

- Resource Exchange Identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You may use an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P1 Resource Types' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view to create or modify a Resource Exchange.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add <ac:link ac:anchor="new"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P3 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link> or <ac:link ac:anchor="existing"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating P3 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link> Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>to fill in a P3 table.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="296727d2-894a-4d95-b61a-d578e1a992f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">new</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add new Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>are added to the table.</li></ol><p> </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f07942c7-23e4-4010-b873-bb1d2373f2fc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">existing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add an existing Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the P3 diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></strong>dialog opens.</li><li>Select Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>by clicking <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>Identifier, Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.</li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="P1 Resource Types" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view</span></span> to create or modify a Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927317 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00424: Creating P4 Resource Functions diagram

- page_id: `191927317`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927317/Creating+P4+Resource+Functions+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-05-29T08:35:43.140+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P4 Resource Functions
- labels: ['create-p4-resource-functions']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the P4 Resource Functions diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] should be created first.

To create a P4 Resource Functions diagram

1. Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Function Actions using the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.
3. Display the possible Resource Exchanges on every Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.

To display possible ResourceExchanges, do either

- Select Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
- In the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927318 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00425: Creating P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `191927318`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927318/Creating+P4+Resource+Functions+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-20T08:26:55.853+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P4 Resource Functions
- labels: ['create-p4-resource-functions-taxonomy']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P1 Resource Types' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view.
3. Use Is Capable To Perform relationship to connect the Resources and Functions.
4. Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.
5. Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a P4 Resource Functions Taxonomy diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from<span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="P1 Resource Types" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></li><li>Use Is Capable To Perform relationship to connect the Resources and Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L4 Logical Activities" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927328 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00426: Creating P7 diagram

- page_id: `191927328`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927328/Creating+P7+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-20T08:37:30.043+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P7 Physical Data Model
- labels: ['create-p7']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P7 diagram

1. Create Data Elements.
2. Add Properties for Data Elements.
3. Draw Association of Information relationships between Data Elements.

Entities can be linked with Data Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a P7 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Data Elements.</li><li>Add Properties for Data Elements.</li><li>Draw Association of Information relationships between Data Elements.</li></ol><p>Entities can be linked with Data Elements. To relate these elements, use Details relationship.</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927331 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00427: Creating P8 table

- page_id: `191927331`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927331/Creating+P8+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-20T09:02:04.222+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P8 Resource Constraints
- labels: ['create-p8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a P8 table, do either

- Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the **Add New** button. The new row in the table is created.Select a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, Data Element, Entity Item, Resource Exchange ). Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).
- Add the existing Resource Constraints by clicking the Add Existing button and select existing Resource Constraints from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a P8 table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button. The new row in the table is created.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6ce018c8-0c16-450f-b89b-b2c7c9909dd7"> S</ac:inline-comment-marker></span>elect a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, </span>Data Element, Entity Item, Resource Exchange<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">). Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).</span></li><li>Add the existing Resource Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing </strong> button and select existing Resource Constraints from the model.</li></ul>
````

## PAGE 00429: Creating Pr diagram

- page_id: `191927335`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927335/Creating+Pr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-12-08T10:51:59.824+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > Pr Configuration Management
- labels: ['create-pr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create Pr diagram

truenone

##### Create new Version Of Configuration

To create new Version Of Configuration in Pr diagram

1. Click the **Add New** button and select Version of Configuration.
2. Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.

##### Assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration

To assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select **Resource**.
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
3. Click Finish when you are done. The resource is assigned for Version Of Configuration in specified time frame. [ATTACHMENT filename='version_of_configuration.png']

The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928432 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00430: Creating predefined relation map

- page_id: `191928432`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928432/Creating+predefined+relation+map
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-08-29T13:42:44.418+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Managing traceability and impact analysis > Traceability relation maps
- labels: ['create-predefined-relation-map']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create predefined relation maps for the selected context elements.

To create the predefined relation map for the context element, do either

- Right-click the element, select Create Diagram and under the UAF Analysis Diagrams , and choose the relation map you want to create.
- Right-click the element, select Related Elements and then choose the relation map you want to create.

You can also create the predefined relation maps in the following way:

- In the main toolbar, click the Create Diagram button, under the UAF Analysis Diagrams group, choose the relation map you want to create, and then select the context element.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create predefined relation maps for the selected context elements.</p><p>To create the predefined relation map for the context element, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click the element, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">and under the </span> <strong>UAF Analysis Diagrams</strong>, and choose the relation map you want to create.</li><li>Right-click the element, select <strong>Related Elements</strong> and then choose the relation map you want to create.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac9b2656-f75a-48e1-9ee5-3f42d303776d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also create the predefined relation maps in the following way:</p><ul><li>In the main toolbar, click the<strong> Create Diagram</strong> button, under the UAF Analysis Diagrams group, choose the relation map you want to create, and then select the context element.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928336 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00431: Creating Process Flow Diagrams From Compositions or Aggregations Defined in Process Diagrams

- page_id: `191928336`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928336/Creating+Process+Flow+Diagrams+From+Compositions+or+Aggregations+Defined+in+Process+Diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-07-25T13:03:49.743+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1179127095

INLINE

1179127116

INLINE

1179127105

INLINE

When you have a process diagram with the defined aggregation/composition of activities/functions, you can create a process flow diagram for the selected activity/function:

- From the Smart manipulator toolbar.
- From the shortcut menu in the Containment tree.

To create a process flow diagram

1. Do one of the following:
  - In a process diagram pane, click the activity for which you want to create a process flow diagram. In the Smart manipulator toolbar, click the process flow diagram button. [ATTACHMENT filename='process_to_process_flow.png']
  - In the Containment tree, right-click the activity and in the shortcut menu, click Create Diagram and the choose <*view*> Process Flow .
2. The Select Behavior dialog opens. In the open dialog, select which behaviors you want to be created in the process flow diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_behavior_dialog.png']
3. Click OK .

The new process flow diagram is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Note, that only the first level behaviors will be suggested in the **Select Behavior** dialog.

1179127084

INLINE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926535 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00432: Creating PV-1 diagram

- page_id: `191926535`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926535/Creating+PV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-14T07:12:39.960+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Project viewpoint > PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['create-pv-1-diagram']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] before creating a PV-1.

To create a PV-1 diagram

1. Create Actual Projects and Actual Organizations.
2. Associate Actual Projects with Actual Organizations ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] ) or Actual Posts ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] ) responsible for them using Responsible For relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to create an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before creating a PV-1.</p><p>To create a PV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Projects and Actual Organizations.</li><li>Associate Actual Projects with Actual  Organizations (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) or <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Posts </span>(<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) responsible for them using <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Responsible For </span>relationship.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926536 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00433: Creating PV-1 matrix

- page_id: `191926536`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926536/Creating+PV-1+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-01T11:55:50.044+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Project viewpoint > PV-1 Project Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['create-pv-1-matrix', 'build-pv-1-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] before creating a PV-1.

The PV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of actual organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, andActual Posts) for Projects.

The rows in the PV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent actual organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, and Actual Posts) and the columns represent Actual Projects .

To create a PV-1 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, and Actual Posts).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Actual Projects).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to create an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before creating a PV-1.</p><p>The PV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of actual organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, and<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Actual Posts</span>) for Projects.</p><p>The rows in the PV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent actual organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, and Actual Posts) and the columns represent Actual Projects .</p><p>To create a PV-1 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Actual Organizations, Actual Persons, and Actual Posts).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Actual Projects).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926539 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00434: Creating PV-2 diagram

- page_id: `191926539`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926539/Creating+PV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-14T07:15:05.454+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Project viewpoint > PV-2 Project Timelines
- labels: ['create-pv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you choose to create PV-2 diagram, the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** opens.

To create a PV-2 diagram

1. Right-click the PV-2 Package and select Create Diagram > PV-2 Project Timelines .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create PV-2 Project Timelines** check box.

To create a PV-2 diagram manually when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**PV-2 Creation Wizard** is disabled

1. Right-click the PV-2 Package and select Create Diagram > PV-2 Project Timelines .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter . The blank PV-2 Project Timelines diagram is created.

To restore the usage of the**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you choose to create PV-2 diagram, the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body> <strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</p><p>To create a PV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the PV-2 Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>PV-2 Project Timelines</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c77ed02-f212-4ece-8767-9903e864e6c7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> next time, clear the <strong>Show the wizard next time, when I create PV-2 Project Timelines</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a PV-2 diagram manually when <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>PV-2 Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> is disabled</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the PV-2 Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>PV-2 Project Timelines</strong>.</li><li><p>Type the diagram name.</p></li><li>Press <strong>Enter</strong>. <br />The blank <strong>PV-2 Project Timelines</strong> diagram is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be5825d5-3d47-4481-9536-da3fe0194dee"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To restore the usage of the<strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[PV-2 Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UAF</strong> options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard</strong> <strong>each Time Creating New Chart</strong> property value to<em> true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926544 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00435: Creating PV-3 matrix

- page_id: `191926544`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926544/Creating+PV-3+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-09-10T08:36:09.829+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Project viewpoint > PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping
- labels: ['create-pv-3-matrix', 'build-pv-3-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping** describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability.

The rows of this matrix are Actual Projects and the columns are Capabilities.

To create a PV-3 matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Actual Projects).
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix.

By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:

- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.

You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the **Specification** window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>PV-3 Project to Capability Mapping</strong> describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability. </p><p>The rows of this matrix are Actual Projects and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a PV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Actual Projects).</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix. </p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:</span></p><ul><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b006062-f431-4f55-b590-c1c8054e6b8b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the <strong>Specification</strong> window.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="mapping_criteria.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating PV-3 matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926855 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00436: Creating Resource  Exchange in MODAF SV-1 diagram

- page_id: `191926855`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926855/Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+MODAF+SV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-12-16T13:13:44.152+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchange in SV-1 diagram

1. Open the Resource Exchange creation wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Interface and on the smart manipulator, click New Resource Exchange .
  - Select the Association and on the smart manipulator, click New Resource Exchange .
  - In the diagram palette, click the Resource Exchange and connect the resource elements on the diagram pane.
2. In the Resource Exchange creation wizard specify the Resource Exchange Item and choose either Select Existing or Create new Resource Exchange.
3. Specify the direction of the Resource Exchange.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resource Exchange in SV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>creation wizard by doing one of the following:<ul><li>Select the Resource Interface and on the smart manipulator, click <strong>New Resource Exchange </strong>.</li><li>Select the Association and on the smart manipulator, click <strong>New Resource Exchange </strong>.</li><li>In the diagram palette, click the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>and connect the resource elements on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>creation wizard specify the Resource Exchange Item and choose either Select Existing or Create new Resource Exchange.</li><li>Specify the direction of the Resource Exchange.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926860 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00437: Creating Resource Exchange in MODAF SV-2 diagram

- page_id: `191926860`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926860/Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+MODAF+SV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-12-16T10:04:17.063+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-2 Systems Communications Description
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchange in SV-2 diagrams

1. Open the Resource Exchange creation wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Interface and in the smart manipulator, click New Resource Exchange .
  - On the diagram palette, click the Resource Exchange and then click the Resource Interface.
  - On the diagram palette, click the Resource Exchange button and then connect the resource elements on the diagram pane.
2. In the Resource Exchange creation wizard, specify the Resource Exchange Item and choose either Select Existing or Create new Resource Exchange.
3. Specify the direction of the Resource Exchange.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resource Exchange in SV-2 diagrams</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>creation wizard by doing one of the following:<ul><li>Select the Resource Interface and in the smart manipulator, click <strong>New Resource Exchange</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>and then click the Resource Interface.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>button and then connect the resource elements on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>creation wizard, specify the Resource Exchange Item and choose either Select Existing or Create new Resource Exchange.</li><li>Specify the direction of the Resource Exchange.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927275 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00439: Creating Resource Exchange in P1 diagram

- page_id: `191927275`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927275/Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+P1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-11-06T14:56:22.841+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-p1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchange in P1 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Association and on the smart manipulator, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - In the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and connect the Resources on the diagram pane.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager**dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927306 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00440: Creating Resource Exchange in P2i diagram

- page_id: `191927306`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927306/Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+P2i+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-29T09:11:41.613+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P2 Resource Internal Structure
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-p2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchangein P2i diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Exchange or Resource Connector and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and then click the Resource Connector.
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and then connect the Resource elements on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>in P2i diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>or Resource Connector and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="New Resource Exchange " ac:alt="New Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click <ac:image ac:title="Resource Exchange " ac:alt="Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and then click the Resource Connector.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click <ac:image ac:title="Resource Exchange " ac:alt="Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and then connect the Resource elements on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</span></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="adc947e6-0729-46d0-a8c6-b91c63e982f3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00441: Creating Resource Exchange in P4 Resource Functions diagram

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Resource Exchangein the P4 Resource Functions diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow and on the smart manipulator toolbar click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and then click the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow .
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the ResourceExchangesdata.
