wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> </span>by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Function <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Control Flow or Function ac:title="Resource Exchange " ac:alt="Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and then click the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Function <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Control Flow or Function Object Flow</span></span>.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">Exchange</span>, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</span></li><li>Click 2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchangein SV-1 Internal diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and then click the Resource Connector.
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>in SV-1 Internal diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="New Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Resource Exchange "><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> and then click the Resource Connector.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</span></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="135f259e-df6b-4a88-826a-382a79531cef"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926672 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00445: Creating Resource Exchange in SV-2 diagram

- page_id: `191926672`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926672/Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+SV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-11-06T14:55:44.068+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-sv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchangein SV-2 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of resources and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, connect resources.
  - Click the Control relationship and connect Posts or Organizations with Posts, Organizations, Natural Resources, Software, Resource Artifact, Technology.
  - Click the Command relationship and connect Posts, Organizations.
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager**dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>in SV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Association  </span>between a pair of resources and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="New Resource Exchange" ac:alt="New Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Resource Exchange" ac:alt="Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> and on the diagram pane, connect<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> resources.</span></li><li>Click the Control relationship and connect Posts or Organizations with Posts, Organizations, Natural Resources, Software, Resource Artifact, Technology.</li><li>Click the Command relationship and connect Posts, Organizations.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="edd9cc73-f3d1-4686-a619-303c55fccc6e"><ac:rich-text-body>Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a2aa563e-be67-452b-ae59-f97ff2723412"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span class="confluence-link">You can use <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><strong><span class="confluence-link">Resource Exchange Manager</span> </strong>dialog</span></span> </ac:link-body></ac:link> to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eefa864d-0fcb-466e-ae3b-8d212a8147f6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926682 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00447: Creating Resource Exchange in SV-3 matrix

- page_id: `191926682`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926682/Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+SV-3+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T10:52:40.266+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix
- labels: ['add-resource-interaction-in-sv-3-matrix', 'delete-resource-interaction-from-sv-3-matrix', 'manage-sv-3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new Resource Exchange

1. Right-click the intersection in the matrix and select Add New Resource Exchange .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard .
3. Click Finish when you are done. A new Resource Exchange appears in the selected intersection.

To remove an existing Resource Exchange

1. Right-click the filled intersection.
2. Under the Delete section, choose the Resource Exchange you want to remove from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a new Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the intersection in the matrix and select <strong>Add New Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />A new Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>appears in the selected intersection.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove an existing Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the filled intersection.</li><li>Under the <strong>Delete</strong> section, choose the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>you want to remove from the model.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926688 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00448: Creating Resource Exchange in SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram

- page_id: `191926688`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926688/Creating+Resource+Exchange+in+SV-4+Systems+Functionality+Flow+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-05-29T08:35:27.584+02:00`

- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-sv-4-systems-functionality-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Resource Exchangein the SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow and on the smart manipulator toolbar click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - On the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and then click Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use ****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the ResourceExchangesdata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> </span>by doing one of the following:<span> </span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow and on the smart manipulator toolbar click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="New Resource Exchange" ac:alt="New Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span>. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">On the diagram palette, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Resource Exchange" ac:alt="Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_exchange.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span> and then click Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.</span></li></ul></li><li>Specify the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">Exchange</span>, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</span></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd8eb90b-faef-480b-bacf-c6003f9fc179"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use <strong><span class="confluence-link"> </span></strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong><span class="confluence-link">Resource Exchange</span> Manager</strong></span> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the <span class="confluence-link">Resource </span><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span></span></span>data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and then click the Resource Message.
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and connect the Resource lifeline elements on the diagram pane.
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927033 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00450: Creating Resource Exchanges in NSV-2 diagram

- page_id: `191927033`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927033/Creating+Resource+Exchanges+in+NSV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-12-16T13:12:23.367+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Systems Viewpoint > NSV-2 System Communications Description
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchange in NSV-2 diagrams

1. Open the Resource Exchange creation wizard by doing one of the following:
  - Select the Resource Interface and in the smart manipulator, click New Resource Exchange .
  - On the diagram palette, click the Resource Exchange and then click the Resource Interface.
  - On the diagram palette, click the Resource Exchange button and then connect the resource elements on the diagram pane.
2. In the Resource Exchange creation wizard, specify the Resource Exchange Item and choose either Select Existing or Create new Resource Exchange.
3. Specify the direction of the Resource Exchange.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Resource Exchange in NSV-2 diagrams</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>creation wizard by doing one of the following:<ul><li>Select the Resource Interface and in the smart manipulator, click <strong>New Resource Exchange</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>and then click the Resource Interface.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>button and then connect the resource elements on the diagram pane.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Resource Exchange </strong>creation wizard, specify the Resource Exchange Item and choose either Select Existing or Create new Resource Exchange.</li><li>Specify the direction of the Resource Exchange.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926664 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00451: Creating Resource Exchanges in SV-1 diagram

- page_id: `191926664`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926664/Creating+Resource+Exchanges+in+SV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-11-06T14:56:11.373+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-1 Systems Interface Description
- labels: ['create-resource-interaction-in-sv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Resource Exchangein SV-1 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of resources and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and on the diagram pane, connect resources.
  - Click the Control relationship and connect Posts or Organizations with Posts, Organizations, Natural Resources, Software, Resource Artifact, Technology.
  - Click the Command relationship and connect Posts, Organizations.
2. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager**dialog **** to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.

****

/></ri:attachment></ac:image> and on the diagram pane, connect<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> resources.</span></li><li>Click the Control relationship and connect Posts or Organizations with Posts, Organizations, Natural Resources, Software, Resource Artifact, Technology.</li><li>Click the Command relationship and connect Posts, Organizations.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927200 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00452: Creating S1 diagram

- page_id: `191927200`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927200/Creating+S1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-11T13:19:28.022+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: ['create-s1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create S1 just before finishing an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L1 Node Types' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P1 Resource Types' space='UAF12P2024xR2']. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.

To create an S1 diagram

1. Create Service Interfaces.
2. Connect Service Interfaces using Generalizations (general-specific relationships).

The S1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create S1 just before finishing an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L1 Node Types" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L1 view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="P1 Resource Types" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P1 view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.</p><p>To create an S1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Interfaces.</li><li>Connect Service Interfaces using Generalizations (general-specific relationships).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b2d16256-bd5e-4a9e-b2f3-1e818e46fc67"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The S1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927207 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00453: Creating S2 diagram

- page_id: `191927207`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927207/Creating+S2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-29T08:59:25.916+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S2 Service Structure
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an S2 Service Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the S2 package and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**S2 Service Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for**S2 Service Structure,**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Service element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create a Service in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Service and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating S2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To create a Service in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the needed package, and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Service and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='S2_drag_service.png']

Service

To create the elements from other resources (e.g., Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Service (or another appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='MD2022xR2'].

##### Connecting the Services

When a Service is created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating S2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating S2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the ServiceRoles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity are shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element that will be the Aggregation End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Association** and choose **Directed Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='S2_edit_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2022xR2'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating S2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='S2_specify_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the Specification window of the role element.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating S2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. Using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you use these notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you use these notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2022xR2'] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='MD2022xR2']** dialog, select **Association.** If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the **Resource Association** also.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='S2_display_related.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating S2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.
2. Select the element and click the Compartments, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2022xR2'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='S2_compartment.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such a case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating S2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2022xR2'] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2022xR2'] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='MD2022xR2'] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='S2_display_parts.png']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating S2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f96904b-939d-4d80-9234-c113de640666"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating a diagram</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an S2 Service Structure diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment Tree, select the S2 package and do one of the following:</span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> S2 Service Structure</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="170" ac:width="310"><ri:attachment ri:filename="s2_new_diagram)containment_tree.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for<strong> S2 Service Structure, </strong>and select it.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating an element</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Service element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.<br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a Service in a diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the diagram palette, click the Service and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="244" ac:width="562"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_create_service.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a Service in a Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment tree, right-click the needed package, and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong>. Search for the Service and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="278" ac:width="323"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_new_service_from_containment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="220" ac:width="761"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_drag_service.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7d01530-058c-4324-a12b-1096609019f2"><ac:rich-text-body>The structure of each <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Service </span>can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="S2 Service Internal Structure" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g., Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Service </span>(or another appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b78b18b-abe1-4fe3-a29c-3e9af9c1ac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connecting the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Services</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When a Service is created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.</span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>To connect the elements with the Composition relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the Smart Manipulator, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> which appears after you select the element, choose</span> Directed Composition.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0780fc43-c960-4eb5-907e-da21eeb36ba3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_direct_composition.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="306" ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_connect_services.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Service </span>Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity are shown.</p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="298" ac:width="517"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_multiplicity.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element that will be the Aggregation End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the diagram palette, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">click the black arrow near the <strong>Association</strong> and choose <strong>Directed Aggregation</strong></span>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="368" ac:width="387"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_directed_aggregation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity</h3><p>To change the role name</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Name</strong>. The default name will be displayed.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="172" ac:width="526"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_edit_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. </p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>To specify multiplicity</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.</li><li>At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="711" ac:width="494"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_specify_multiplicity.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the Specification window of the role element.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><h3>Displaying created structure</h3><p>If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:</p><ul><li>Relationship (direct) Notation. Using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.</li><li>Attribute Compartment Notation. If you use these notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.</li><li>Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you use these notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Association.</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8fe10c2-93e5-4905-aae2-8a0324b428b4"><ac:rich-text-body>If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the <strong>Resource Association</strong> also.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="882" ac:width="578"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_display_related.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Attribute Compartment Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.</li><li>Select the element and click the Compartments, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_compartment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Compartments menu, select <strong>Attributes</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="711a7e71-ce7a-474a-a540-955126beae32"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such a case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Structure (Nested elements) Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the element, click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and choose <strong>Structure</strong>.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="786" ac:width="770"><ri:attachment ri:filename="S2_display_parts.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating S2 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927245 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00454: Creating S4 Service Functions diagram

- page_id: `191927245`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927245/Creating+S4+Service+Functions+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-20T07:12:29.448+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S4 Service Functions
- labels: ['create-s4-service-functions']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an S4 Service Functions diagram

1. Create Service Function Actions or drag the them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Service Function Actions using the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an S4 Service Functions diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Function Actions or drag the them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect the Service Function Actions using the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927246 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00455: Creating S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `191927246`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927246/Creating+S4+Service+Functions+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-20T07:12:45.963+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S4 Service Functions
- labels: ['create-s4-service-functions-taxonomy']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram

1. Create Service Functions.
2. Use Compositions or Aggregations (whole-part relationships) to connect the Service Functions.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an S4 Service Functions Taxonomy diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions or Aggregations (whole-part relationships) to connect the Service Functions.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927256 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00456: Creating S8 table

- page_id: `191927256`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927256/Creating+S8+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-20T07:25:17.953+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S8 Service Policy
- labels: ['create-s8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create S8 table, do either

- Add a new Service Policy by clicking the Add New button and select a constrained Service Interface. Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example, English or OCL).
- Add an existing Service Policy by clicking the Add Existing button and select a Service Policies.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create S8 table<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">, do either</span><span> </span></p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Service Policy by clicking the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select a constrained Service Interface. Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example, English or OCL).</li><li>Add an existing Service Policy by clicking the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and select a Service Policies.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926817 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00457: Creating SOV-1 diagram

- page_id: `191926817`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926817/Creating+SOV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T14:12:12.224+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint > MODAF. SOV-1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create SOV-1 just before finishing an OV-2 or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the SV-1 or SV-2 views. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.

To create a SOV-1 diagram

1. Create Service Interfaces.
2. Connect Service Interfaces using generalizations (general-specific relationships).

The SOV-1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create SOV-1 just before finishing an OV-2 or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the SV-1 or SV-2 views. It depends on the usage of Service and Request Ports in the architecture.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SOV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Interfaces.</li><li>Connect Service Interfaces using generalizations (general-specific relationships).</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The SOV-1 view also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Interfaces mapping.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926836 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00458: Creating SOV-5 Service Functionality Description diagram

- page_id: `191926836`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926836/Creating+SOV-5+Service+Functionality+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T14:30:22.804+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint > MODAF. SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A SOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Service Functionality Description diagram.
2. Service Functionality Flow diagram.

To create a SOV-5 Product, you need to create a Service Functionality Description diagram first.

To create a Service Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Service Functions.
2. Draw compositions or aggregations (whole-part relationships) between the Service Functions.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A SOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Service Functionality Description diagram.</li><li>Service Functionality Flow diagram.</li></ol><p>To create a SOV-5 Product, you need to create a Service Functionality Description diagram first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Service Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Functions.</li><li>Draw compositions or aggregations (whole-part relationships) between the Service Functions.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926837 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00459: Creating SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow diagram

- page_id: `191926837`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926837/Creating+SOV-5+Service+Functionality+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T14:31:49.876+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint > MODAF. SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The SOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:

1. Service Functionality Description diagram.
2. Service Functionality Flow diagram.

Before creating the Service Functionality Flow diagram, the Service Functionality Description diagram must be created first.

To create a Service Functionality Flow diagram

1. Create Service Function Actions.
2. Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service Function Edges.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The SOV-5 view consists of these two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Service Functionality Description diagram.</li><li>Service Functionality Flow diagram.</li></ol><p>Before creating the Service Functionality Flow diagram, the Service Functionality Description diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Service Functionality Flow diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Function Actions.</li><li>Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service Function Edges.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926730 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00460: Creating StdV-1 table

- page_id: `191926730`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926730/Creating+StdV-1+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T11:45:12.901+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Standards viewpoint > StdV-1 Standards Profile
- labels: ['create-stdv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an StdV-1 table, do either

- Create a new element: 1) Click the Add New button. 2) From the drop-down list select the desired element. 3) In the Select Owner dialog choose the owner and click OK . 4) In the element's Specification window type its name and click Close . The element appears under the System element column. 5) Select the cell under the Standard/Policy column and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] . 6) In the Select Standard dialog select one or more Standards, Protocols, or Protocol Stacks.
- Add an existing element. 
 1) Click the**Add Existing** button. 
 2) In the **Select UAF element** dialog select desired elements and click **OK**. The element appears under the **System element** column. 
 3) Select the cell under the **Standard/Policy** column and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . 
 4) In the **Select Standard** dialog select one or more Standards, Protocols, or Protocol Stacks.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an StdV-1 table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Create a new element:<br /> 1) Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button.<br /> 2) From the drop-down list select the desired element.<br /> 3) In the <strong>Select Owner</strong> dialog choose the owner and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /> 4) In the element's Specification window type its name and click <strong>Close</strong>. The element appears under the <strong>System element</strong> column.<br /> 5) Select the cell under the <strong>Standard/Policy</strong> column and click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> .<br /> 6) In the <strong>Select Standard</strong> dialog select one or more Standards, Protocols, or Protocol Stacks.<span> </span></li><li><p>Add an existing element.<br /> 1) Click the<strong> Add Existing</strong> button.<br /> 2) In the <strong>Select UAF element</strong> dialog select desired elements and click <strong>OK</strong>. The element appears under the <strong>System element</strong> column.<br /> 3) Select the cell under the <strong>Standard/Policy</strong> column and click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> .<br /> 4) In the <strong>Select Standard</strong> dialog select one or more Standards, Protocols, or Protocol Stacks.</p></li></ul><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926733 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00461: Creating StdV-2 table

- page_id: `191926733`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926733/Creating+StdV-2+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-11-19T12:52:40.580+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Standards viewpoint > StdV-2 Standards Forecast
- labels: ['create-stdv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658487619

1658487630

1658487620

To create an StdV-2 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658487617

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
