<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928506 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00462: Creating structure models by behavior models

- page_id: `191928506`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928506/Creating+structure+models+by+behavior+models
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:02.479+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > UAF Behavior to structure synchronization
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A structure model can be automatically built according to the existing behavior model. Once the command is initiated, the structure model, including missing Ports, Interfaces, Connectors, and Exchanges, is created. Additionally, elements are displayed in the newly-created Internal Connectivity Diagram. As a result, there is no need to manually define the communication in an Internal Connectivity Diagram once again if you have already defined the behavior model using a Process Flow diagram.

To create a structure model by the behavior model

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] , right-click an Operational Agent, Service Performer, or Resource Performer.
  - On the structure diagram pane, right-click an Operational Agent, Service Performer, or Resource Performershape.
2. In the open shortcut menu, select Tools > Create <viewpoint name> Internal Connectivity Diagram by Behavior Model . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_internal_connectivity_diagram_by_begavior_model.png']

A structure model is automatically created according to the defined behavior model, and compatible elements are displayed on the <viewpoint name> Internal Connectivity Diagram pane.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A structure model can be automatically built according to the existing behavior model. Once the command is initiated, the structure model, including missing Ports, Interfaces, Connectors, and Exchanges, is created. Additionally, elements are displayed in the newly-created Internal Connectivity Diagram. As a result, there is no need to manually define the communication in an Internal Connectivity Diagram once again if you have already defined the behavior model using a Process Flow diagram.  </p><p><br /></p><p><span>To create a structure model by the behavior model</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span>, right-click an Operational Agent, Service Performer, or Resource Performer. </span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the structure diagram pane, right-click an <span>Operational Agent, Service Performer, or Resource Performer </span>shape.</p></li></ul></li><li>In the open shortcut menu, select<strong> Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Create &lt;viewpoint name&gt; Internal Connectivity Diagram by Behavior Model</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_internal_connectivity_diagram_by_begavior_model.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating structure models by behavior models" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>A structure model is automatically created according to the defined behavior model, and compatible elements are displayed on the &lt;viewpoint name&gt; Internal Connectivity Diagram pane. </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926745 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00463: Creating StV-1 diagram

- page_id: `191926745`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926745/Creating+StV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T12:41:14.875+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > MODAF. StV-1 Enterprise Vision
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The StV-1 are the one of the firstly created products in MODAF architecture.

To create a StV-1 diagram

1. Create a Whole Life Enterprise.
2. Add Structural and Temporal parts to the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking the Temporal Part or Structural Part button and click on the Whole-Life Enterprise body.
3. Specify the Types for the created parts (the types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements).

Additionally Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.

To fully complete the StV-1, the StV-2 should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. To specify the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises, exhibits relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The StV-1 are the one of the firstly created products in MODAF architecture.</p><p>To create a StV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Whole Life Enterprise.</li><li>Add Structural and Temporal parts to the Whole Life Enterprise by clicking the Temporal Part or Structural Part button and click on the Whole-Life Enterprise body.</li><li>Specify the Types for the created parts (the types for both parts are the Enterprise Phase elements).</li></ol><p>Additionally Enterprise Goals and Visions can be created and added to the Enterprise Phase’s or Whole Life Enterprise’s goal and vision property.</p><p>To fully complete the StV-1, the StV-2 should be modeled in order to create Capabilities that Enterprise Phase and Whole Life Enterprise exhibit. To specify the relation between Capabilities and Enterprises, exhibits relationship.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926748 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00464: Creating StV-2 diagram

- page_id: `191926748`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926748/Creating+StV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T12:43:56.430+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > MODAF. StV-2 Capability Taxonomy
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The StV-2 should be modeled just before the StV-1 is completed.

To create a StV-2 diagram

1. Create Capabilities.
2. Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or aggregations (whole-part relationships).

You can also link Capabilities with the Operational and System elements using exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibitionfill in the Exhibits relationship property 
*environmentConditions*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The StV-2 should be modeled just before the StV-1 is completed.</p><p>To create a StV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Capabilities.</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Generalizations (general - specific relationships) or aggregations (whole-part relationships).</li></ol><p>You can also link Capabilities with the Operational and System elements using exhibits relationship. To specify environmental requirements for Capability exhibitionfill in the Exhibits relationship property<br /><em>environmentConditions</em>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926754 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00465: Creating StV-4 diagram

- page_id: `191926754`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926754/Creating+StV-4+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T12:54:34.618+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > MODAF. StV-4 Capability Dependencies
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The StV-4 is created just after the StV-2 is completed.

To create a StV-4 diagram

1. Create or reuse the Capabilities from StV-2 (recommended).
2. Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The StV-4 is created just after the StV-2 is completed.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a StV-4 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create or reuse the Capabilities from StV-2 (recommended).</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926663 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00466: Creating SV-1 diagram

- page_id: `191926663`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926663/Creating+SV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T10:40:03.048+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-1 Systems Interface Description
- labels: ['create-sv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The SV-1 diagram requires that the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2']OV-2 diagram should be completed.

To create an SV-1 diagram

1. Create resources.
2. Connect resources using Association.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Associations.

The SV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and resources mapping the provided and required services. You can model internal structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] diagram.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34fcee1c-4c5e-4f77-a936-469db6216cfb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The SV-1 diagram requires that the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">OV-2</span> diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link> should be completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an SV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create resources.</li><li>Connect resources using Association.</li><li>Create Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>flowing via<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Associations.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The SV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and resources mapping the provided and required services.</span> You can model internal structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1 System Interface Internal Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f25cfe69-86d4-405b-a1f1-7da43fb573ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926668 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00467: Creating SV-1 Internal diagram

- page_id: `191926668`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926668/Creating+SV-1+Internal+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T10:42:42.222+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description
- labels: ['create-sv-1-internal']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The SV-1 Internal diagram requires that the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-2 Internal diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']OV-2 Internal diagram should be completed.

To create an SV-1 Internal diagram

1. Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.
2. Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.

You can model internal structure of each Resource Role directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] diagram.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34fcee1c-4c5e-4f77-a936-469db6216cfb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The SV-1 Internal diagram requires that the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating OV-2 Internal diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">OV-2</span> Internal diagram</ac:link-body></ac:link></span> should be completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create an SV-1 Internal diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.</li><li>Associate Resource Roles using Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connectors.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.</span></li></ol><p> You can model internal structure of each Resource Role directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Internal Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1 System Interface Internal Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f25cfe69-86d4-405b-a1f1-7da43fb573ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the SV-1 has been completed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926718 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00468: Creating SV-10a table

- page_id: `191926718`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926718/Creating+SV-10a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T11:40:11.701+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-10a Systems Rules Model
- labels: ['add-resource-constraint-in-sv-10a', 'create-sv-10a']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an SV-10a table, do either

- Click the Add New button to add a new Resource Constraint. The new row in the table is created. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the cell and in the **Select Subject Of Resource Constraint** dialog choose Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization , Post, Function, Data Element, Resource Exchange.
- Click the Add Existing button to add an existing Resource Constraints in the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an SV-10a table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New </strong>button to add a new Resource Constraint. The new row in the table is created.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> Click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the cell and in the <strong>Select Subject Of Resource Constraint</strong> dialog choose <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Resource Artifact,</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Software, Capability Configuration, Organization</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">, Post, Function, Data Element, Resource Exchange</span>.</span></li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button to add an existing Resource Constraints in the model.</li></ul><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926671 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00469: Creating SV-2 diagram

- page_id: `191926671`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926671/Creating+SV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T10:48:03.150+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-sv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to complete a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] before creating SV-2.

To create a SV-2 diagram

1. Create resources or reuse them from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
2. Connect resources using Associations.
3. Create Resource Interfaces.
4. Create Resource Ports.
5. Specify the types for the Resource Ports. The type of the Resource Port can be only Resource Interface and its subtypes.

SV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular resources. You can model the structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to complete a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> before creating SV-2.</p><p>To create a SV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create resources or reuse them from <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Connect resources using Associations.</li><li>Create Resource Interfaces.</li><li>Create Resource Ports.</li><li><p>Specify the types for the Resource Ports.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8d06663-14f6-438b-8ed6-a66214266957"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The type of the Resource Port can be only Resource Interface and its subtypes.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">SV-2 product allows to show the provided and required services by particular resources. You can model the structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></span></span> diagram.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926676 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00470: Creating SV-2 Internal diagram

- page_id: `191926676`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926676/Creating+SV-2+Internal+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T10:50:29.224+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-sv-2-internal']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an SV-2 Internal diagram

1. Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.
2. Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.

You can model internal structure of each Resource Role directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an SV-2 Internal diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.</li><li>Associate Resource Roles using Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connectors.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.</span></li></ol><p>You can model internal structure of each Resource Role directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Internal Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2 System Internal Resource Flow Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926681 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00471: Creating SV-3 matrix

- page_id: `191926681`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926681/Creating+SV-3+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:53.608+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-3 Systems-Systems Matrix
- labels: ['create-sv-3-matrix', 'build-sv-3-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Systems-Systems Matrix (SV-3) shows how systems interact to each otherdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2'].SV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource Exchangesand their headers represent Resources.

Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource Exchangebetween resources. SV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.

To create a SV-3 matrix

1. Specify the **Row Scope** (Resources).
2. Specify the **Column Scope** (Resources).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Systems-Systems Matrix (SV-3) <span>shows how systems interact to each other </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span>SV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource <span>Exchanges </span>and their headers represent Resources.</p><p>Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>between resources. SV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.</p><p>To create a SV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Resources).</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Resources).</span></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926868 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00472: Creating SV-4 Functionality Description diagram

- page_id: `191926868`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926868/Creating+SV-4+Functionality+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-18T06:00:58.512+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create SV-4, make sure there are OV-5 and SV-1 created in the architecture.

The SV-4 view consists of two diagrams:

1. Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.
2. Functionality Description Flow diagram.This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.

You must create the Functionality Description diagram before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram.

To create a SV-4 Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from SV-1, SV-2.
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between the Resources and Functions.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between the Functions if necessary.
5. Draw an Implements relationship between the Functions and Operational Activities from OV-5.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create SV-4, make sure there are OV-5 and SV-1 created in the architecture.</p><p>The SV-4 view consists of two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.</li><li>Functionality Description Flow diagram.This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.</li></ol><p>You must create the Functionality Description diagram before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SV-4 Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) Resources from SV-1, SV-2.</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between the Resources and Functions.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Draw an Implements relationship between the Functions and Operational Activities from OV-5.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926869 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00473: Creating SV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram

- page_id: `191926869`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926869/Creating+SV-4+Functionality+Description+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-12-16T10:12:33.632+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create SV-4, make sure there are OV-5 and SV-1 created in the architecture.

The SV-4 view consists of two diagrams:

1. Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.
2. Functionality Description Flow diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.

Before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram, the Functionality Description diagram must be created first.

To create a SV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram

1. Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.
2. Connect the Function Actions using the Function Edges.
3. Display the possible Resource Exchanges on every Function Edge.

To display possible Resource Exchanges on selected Activity Edge, click smart manipulator named “Resource Exchange” that appears when Function Edge is selected. You will be able to select possible to realize Resource Exchange. Another way to invoke the Resource Exchange selection Dialog is to click on the “Resource Exchange” button in the Diagram toolbar and to click on the Function Edge.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create SV-4, make sure there are OV-5 and SV-1 created in the architecture.</p><p>The SV-4 view consists of two diagrams:</p><ol><li>Functionality Description diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description hierarchies.</li><li>Functionality Description Flow diagram. This diagram represents Functionality Description flows.</li></ol><p>Before creating the Functionality Description Flow diagram, the Functionality Description diagram must be created first.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SV-4 Functionality Description Flow diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Either create Function Actions or drag the Functions from the Containment tree directly to the diagram.</li><li>Connect the Function Actions using the Function Edges.</li><li>Display the possible Resource Exchanges on every Function Edge.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To display possible Resource Exchanges on selected Activity Edge, click smart manipulator named “Resource Exchange” that appears when Function Edge is selected. You will be able to select possible to realize Resource Exchange. Another way to invoke the Resource Exchange selection Dialog is to click on the “Resource Exchange” button in the Diagram toolbar and to click on the Function Edge.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926686 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00474: Creating SV-4 Systems Functionality Description diagram

- page_id: `191926686`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926686/Creating+SV-4+Systems+Functionality+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T11:01:55.078+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-4 Systems Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-sv-4-systems-functionality-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SV-4 diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] diagrams should be created first.

To create a SV-4 Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) resources from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].
3. Use Is Capable To Perform r elationship to connect the resources and Functions.
4. Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.
5. Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f52e3b83-f45c-4cd2-ac62-09f325eff963"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the SV-4 diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To create a SV-4 Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Functions.</li><li>Create or reuse (recommended) resources from<span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li>Use <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Is Capable To Perform r</span>elationship to connect the resources and Functions.</li><li>Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Functions if necessary.</li><li>Use an Implements relationship to connect the Functions and Operational Activities from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926687 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00475: Creating SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram

- page_id: `191926687`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926687/Creating+SV-4+Systems+Functionality+Flow+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T11:02:08.961+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-4 Systems Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-sv-4-systems-functionality-flow-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram, the SV-4 Systems Functionality Description diagram should be created first.

To create a SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram

1. Create Function Actions or drag them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Function Actions using the Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.
3. Create Resource Exchanges on every Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4b36e701-10bd-41e6-a4db-1527fbf1fbf7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before creating the SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram, the SV-4 <span class="confluence-link">Systems Functionality Description diagram</span> should be created first.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To create a SV-4 Systems Functionality Flow Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Function Actions or drag them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect the Function Actions using the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link" style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">Create <span class="confluence-link">Resource Exchanges</span> </span> on every Function Control Flow or Function Object Flow</span>.</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926692 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00476: Creating SV-5a matrix

- page_id: `191926692`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926692/Creating+SV-5a+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:53.783+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-5a Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix
- labels: ['create-sv-5a-matrix', 'build-sv-5a-matrix', 'relate-function-to-operational-activity-in-sv-5a-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix (SV-5a) addresses thedirect and indirect (using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2'])linkage between Functions described in SV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5.

The rows of the SV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.

To create a SV-5 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Functions).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.

To map a Function to an Operational Activity, do either

- Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.
- Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Implements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Operational Activity to Systems Function Traceability Matrix (SV-5a) <span>addresses the </span><span>direct and indirect (using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) </span><span>linkage between Functions described in SV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5.</span></p><p>The rows of the SV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SV-5 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Functions).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p>Functions implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.</p><p> </p><p>To map a Function to an Operational Activity, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.</li><li>Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Implements.</li></ul><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926696 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00477: Creating SV-5b matrix

- page_id: `191926696`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926696/Creating+SV-5b+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:53.878+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-5b Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix
- labels: ['create-sv-5b-matrix', 'build-sv-5b-matrix', 'relate-function-to-operational-activity-in-sv-5b-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix (SV-5b) describes thedirect and indirect (using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2']) mapping between the Systems and Operational Activities.

The rows of the SV-5b matrix represent resourcesand the columns represent Operational Activities.

To create a SV-5b matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Resources Artifacts).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Resources implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Operational Activity to Systems Traceability Matrix (SV-5b) <span>describes the </span><span>direct and indirect (using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) m</span><span>apping between the Systems and Operational Activities.</span></p><p>The rows of the SV-5b matrix represent <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">resources </span>and the columns represent Operational Activities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SV-5b matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> (Resources Artifacts).</span></li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Resources implements Operational Activities using the Implements relationship.</span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926701 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00478: Creating SV-6 matrix

- page_id: `191926701`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926701/Creating+SV-6+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-10-20T13:06:02.652+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix
- labels: ['add-resource-interaction-in-sv-6', 'create-sv-6']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add new or existing Resource Exchanges, Commands, and Controlsto fill in an SV-6 matrix.

To add new Resource Exchange type

1. In the SV-6 matrix toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Resource Exchanges type are added to the matrix.

To add an existing Resource Exchange type

1. In the SV-6 matrix toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource **Exchange** dialog opens.
2. Click + button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:
  - Resource Exchange
  - Command
  - Control
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchange types are added to the matrix.

- Resource Exchange Identifier, Resource Exchanges Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the matrix.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You may use an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view to create or modify a Resource Exchange.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges, Commands, and Controls </span>to fill in an SV-6 matrix.</p><p> </p><p>To add new Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange type</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the SV-6 matrix toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges type </span>are added to the matrix.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add an existing Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange type</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the SV-6 matrix toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource </strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Exchange</strong> </span>dialog opens.</li><li>Click <strong>+</strong> button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:<br /><ul><li>Resource Exchange</li><li>Command</li><li>Control</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange types </span>are added to the matrix.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd42d46f-003d-42ce-ae5f-8b5fda76b70e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>Identifier, Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the matrix.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.</li><li>You may use an <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> view</span></span> to create or modify a Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926707 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00479: Creating SV-8 diagram

- page_id: `191926707`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926707/Creating+SV-8+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T11:36:13.547+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-8 Systems Evolution Description
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create SV-8 diagram

truenone

##### Create new Version Of Configuration

To create new Version Of Configuration in SV-8 diagram

1. Click the Add New button and select Version of Configuration.
2. Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.

##### Assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration

To assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select Resource .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Assignment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
3. Click Finish when you are done. The resource is assigned for Version Of Configuration in specified time frame. [ATTACHMENT filename='version_of_configuration.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create SV-8 diagram</p><hr /><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a821551c-c5dd-447f-a321-7fe7999c5434"><ac:parameter ac:name="outline">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="style">none</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3 class="confluence-link">Create new Version Of Configuration</h3><p>To create new Version Of Configuration in SV-8 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select Version of Configuration.</li><li>Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.</li></ol><h3>Assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration</h3><p>To assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select <strong>Resource</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Assignment Wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The resource is assigned for Version Of Configuration in specified time frame.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="version_of_configuration.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926713 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00480: Creating SV-9 table

- page_id: `191926713`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926713/Creating+SV-9+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-11-19T12:49:23.799+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Systems viewpoint > SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast
- labels: ['create-sv-9']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658334558

1658334569

1658334559

To create a SV-9 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding new subjects of forecast

To add new subjects of forecast

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

##### Adding existing subjects of forecast

To add existing subjects of forecast

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual **Strategic**Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658334556

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926600 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00481: Creating SvcV-1 diagram

- page_id: `191926600`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926600/Creating+SvcV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T07:56:07.118+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint > SvcV-1 Services Context Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create an SvcV-1 just before finishing an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] views.

To create a SvcV-1 diagram

1. Create Service Specification.
2. Associate Service Specification usingAssociation relationships.

The SvcV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service Specifications mapping and Service Specificationsinteractions with the system resources.You can model each Service Specifications internal structure directly in the compartment area of the element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view as soon as the SvcV-1 has been completed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create an SvcV-1 just before finishing an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> views.</p><p>To create a SvcV-1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service Specification.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Associate <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service Specification</span> using<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Association relationships</span>.</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br style="color: rgb(51,51,51);" /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The SvcV-1 product also allows you to show the Capabilities and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service Specification</span>s mapping and Service Specifications </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">interactions with the system resources. </span>You can model each <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service Specification</span>s</span> internal structure directly in the compartment area of the element shape or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Internal Description diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the SvcV-1 has been completed.</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926650 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00482: Creating SvcV-10a table

- page_id: `191926650`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926650/Creating+SvcV-10a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T08:38:07.486+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint > SvcV-10a Services Rules Model
- labels: ['create-svcv-10a']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an SvcV-10a table, do either

- Click the Add New button to add a new Resource Constraints. The new row in the table is created. Select constrained element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization , Post, Function, DataElement, Resource Exchange).
- Click the Add Existing button and select Resource Constraint from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926604 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00483: Creating SvcV-2 diagram

- page_id: `191926604`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926604/Creating+SvcV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T08:01:12.952+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint > SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create an SvcV-2 just before finishing an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] views.

To create a SvcV-2 diagram

1. Create Service Specifications and Service Ports.
2. Associate Service Specifications using Associations .
3. Associate Service Ports using Service Connectors.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] view as soon as the SvcV-2 has been completed.

You can model internal structure of each Service Specification directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-2 Internal diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create an SvcV-2 just before finishing an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or in later stages of the architecture just before finishing the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> views.</p><p>To create a SvcV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service Specifications and Service Ports.</li><li>Associate Service Specifications using Associations<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Associate Service Ports using Service Connectors.</span><span> </span></li></ol><p>You can proceed to create <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view as soon as the SvcV-2 has been completed.</p><p>You can model internal structure of each Service Specification directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-2 Internal diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-2 Service Resource Flow Internal Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926605 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00484: Creating SvcV-2 Internal diagram

- page_id: `191926605`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a SvcV-2 Internal diagram

1. Create Service Specification Roles and Service Ports .
2. Associate Service Specification Roles and Service Ports using Service Connectors.

````html
<p>To create a SvcV-2 Internal diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service Specification Roles and Service Ports</span>.</li><li>Associate <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service Specification Roles and Service Ports</span> using Service <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connectors.</span><span> </span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926607 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00485: Creating SvcV-3a matrix

- page_id: `191926607`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926607/Creating+SvcV-3a+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-01T11:57:39.248+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint > SvcV-3a Systems-Services Matrix
- labels: ['create-svcv-3a-matrix', 'build-svcv-3a-matrix']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a SvcV-3a matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Resource Performers).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Service Interfaces).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

SvcV-3a diagram is non editable.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a SvcV-3a matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Resource Performers).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e189cc52-3a56-4d2b-9975-4242057ac736"><ac:rich-text-body><p>SvcV-3a diagram is non editable.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

To create a SvcV-3b matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope ( Service ).
2. Specify the Column Scope ( Service ).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a SvcV-3b matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service</span>).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope </strong>(<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service</span>).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926615 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00487: Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram

- page_id: `191926615`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926615/Creating+SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T08:18:26.452+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint > SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-4-services-functionality-description']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SvcV-4 diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] diagrams should be created first.

To create a SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram

1. Create Service Functions.
2. Create or reuse (recommended) Service Specifications.
3. Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Service Functions if necessary.
4. Use an Implements relationship to connect the Service Functions and Operational Activities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-5 Operational Activity Model' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Before creating the SvcV-4 diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams should be created first.</p><p>To create a SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create Service Functions.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create or reuse (recommended) Service Specifications<span class="confluence-link">.  </span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use Compositions (whole-part relationships) to connect the Service Functions if necessary.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use an Implements relationship to connect the Service  Functions and Operational Activities from <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-5 Operational Activity Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926616 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00488: Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram

- page_id: `191926616`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926616/Creating+SvcV-4+Services+Functionality+Flow+Description+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T08:18:41.522+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint > SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-4-services-functionality-flow-description', 'display-resource-interactions-in-svcv-4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating the SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']SvcV-4 Service Functionality Descriptiondiagram should be created first.

To create a SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram

1. Either create Service Function Actions or drag them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
2. Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service Control Flow or Service Object Flow relationships.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Before creating the SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body>SvcV-4 <span class="confluence-link">Service Functionality Description</span></ac:link-body></ac:link><span class="confluence-link"> diagram</span> should be created first.</p><p>To create a SvcV-4 Services Functionality Flow Description diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Either create Service Function Actions or drag them from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.</li><li>Connect the Service Function Actions using the Service <span>Control Flow or Service <span> </span>Object Flow relationships.</span></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926635 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00489: Creating SvcV-8 diagram

- page_id: `191926635`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926635/Creating+SvcV-8+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T08:32:22.865+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint > SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description
- labels: ['create-svcv-8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a SvcV-8 diagram

truenone

##### Create new Version Of Configuration

To create new Version Of Configuration in SV-8 diagram

1. Click the**Add New**button and selectVersion of Configuration.
2. Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.

##### Define the resource for the Version Of Configuration

To define the resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select **Resource**.
2. In the **Select Resource** dialog do one of the following:
  - Select an existing resource that has milestones defined.
  - Create new resource (described in the following procedure).

To create new resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. In the **Select Resource** dialog switch on the **Creation Mode**.
2. Select package where you want to create a resource.
3. Click the **Create** button, choose a resource category******Security, Resources,**or **Operational**, and select the desired resource. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Specification window of the newly createdresource, type its name.

##### Specify Actual Project Milestones of the resource

Each resource must have two Actual Project Milestone properties defined:

- Version Released At Milestone (defines the start of a period, Kind - InService) .
- Version Withdrawn At Milestone (defines the end of a period, Kind - OutOfService).

To specify Actual Project Milestones of the resource

1. In the Specification window of the newly created resource, specify: 
 - the**Version Released At Milestone** property. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the**Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog and do one of the following: - the **Version Withdrawn At Milestone** property. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the**Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog and do one of the following:
  - Select an existingmilestone defined with kind In Service.
  - Create new milestone(described in the following procedure "[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-8 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']").
  - Select an existingmilestone defined with kind Out Of Service.
  - Create new milestone(described in the following procedure "[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating SvcV-8 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']").
2. Click **Close** > **OK**. The Actual Project Milestone is specified.

In_ServiceTo create newmilestone with kind In Service

1. In the **Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog, switch on the **Creation Mode**.
2. Select package where you want to create anActual Project Milestone.
3. Click **Create**.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Specification window of the newly createdActual Project Milestone specify three properties: [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  1. Name. Type a name of the Actual Project Milestone.
  2. Start Date . Define the start of a period. Select the Date property value celland click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Date and Time Settings** dialog choosea date and click **OK**.
  3. Kind. Select the Kind property value cell an choose amilestone kind: In Service.
5. Click Close > OK .

Out_Of_ServiceTo create newmilestone with kind Out Of Service

1. In the **Select Actual Project Milestone** dialog, switch on the **Creation Mode**.
2. Select package where you want to create anActual Project Milestone.
3. Click **Create**.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. In the Specification window of the newly createdActual Project Milestone specify three properties:
  1. Name. Type a name of the Actual Project Milestone.
  2. Start Date . Define the end of a period. Select the Date property value celland click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Date and Time Settings** dialog choosea date and click **OK**.
  3. Kind. Select the Kind property value cell an choose amilestone kind: Out Of Service. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Click Close > OK .

The result of this procedure is as the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926645 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00490: Creating SvcV-9 table

- page_id: `191926645`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926645/Creating+SvcV-9+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-11-19T12:51:02.051+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint > SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast
- labels: ['create-svcv-9']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658435249

1658435261

1658435250

To create a SvcV-9 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strateic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658435247

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926841 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00491: Creating the SV-10a table

- page_id: `191926841`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926841/Creating+the+SV-10a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-12-16T10:04:57.117+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint > MODAF. SV-10a Resource Constraints Specification
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an SV-10a table, do either

- Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the Create New Resource Constraint button and select a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, Data Element, Resource Exchange), and then fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in a natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).
- Add the Existing Resource Constraints by clicking the Add Existing Resource Constraint button and selecting Resource Constraints .

- You can add and edit constrained elements for every Row in the table.
- You can remove Rows (Resource Constraints) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to see more menus.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an SV-10a table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Resource Constraint by clicking the <strong>Create New Resource Constraint</strong> button and select a constrained Systems Element (Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization, Post, Function, Data Element, Resource Exchange), and then fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in a natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).</li><li>Add the Existing Resource Constraints by clicking the <strong>Add Existing Resource Constraint</strong> button and selecting<strong> Resource Constraints</strong>.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b7381a5-8071-43e0-aad0-e07e2453bdfc"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can add and edit constrained elements for every Row in the table.</li><li>You can remove Rows (Resource Constraints) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to see more menus.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926892 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00493: Creating TV-1 table

- page_id: `191926892`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926892/Creating+TV-1+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T14:35:12.077+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Technical Standards Viewpoint > MODAF. TV-1 Standards Profile
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a TV-1 table, do either

- Add a new UPDM Element by clicking the Add new UPDM Element button and select any UPDM Element (this term means any of the UPDM available elements). Specify the owner for the selected element.
- Add the Existing UPDM elements by clicking the Add Existing UPDM Element button and select the UPDM elements.
- Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular UPDM element must conform to by clicking the … button on any cell in the Standard/Policy column.

- You can remove Rows (UPDM Elements) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to see more menus.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a TV-1 table, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new UPDM Element by clicking the <strong>Add new UPDM Element</strong> button and select any UPDM Element (this term means any of the UPDM available elements). Specify the owner for the selected element.</li><li>Add the Existing UPDM elements by clicking the <strong>Add Existing UPDM</strong> Element button and select the UPDM elements.</li><li>Assign one or more Standards or Protocols to each row that the particular UPDM element must conform to by clicking the<strong> …</strong> button on any cell in the <strong>Standard/Policy</strong> column.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac771b7b-bc23-4d1e-92ac-95be2bc7b64d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can remove Rows (UPDM Elements) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to see more menus.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658721790

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
