mapped to Function + Function Action if the ArchiMate Application Function is related to </span>an element that maps to UAF Resource Performer using one of the following:</p><ul><li>Assignment relation</li><li>Association relation</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">8</td><td colspan="2">Application Service</td><td colspan="1">Resource Artifact</td><td colspan="1">Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>9</td><td colspan="2">Artifact</td><td>Resource Information</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">10</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="2">Assessment</td><td colspan="1"><p>Measurement</p></td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Assessment is mapped to Measurement for Property Set if the ArchiMate Assessment element is related to the element which maps to UAF Property Set using the Association relation.  </td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Measurement Set + Measurement</td><td colspan="1"><p>The ArchiMate Assessment is mapped to Measurement Set with Measurement if the ArchiMate Assessment element <strong>IS NOT</strong> related to the element which maps to UAF Property Set using the Association relation.</p><p><strong>Note</strong>. UAF Measurement Set and Measurement has the same name as the ArchiMate Assessment element.</p></td></tr><tr><td>11</td><td colspan="2">Business Actor</td><td>Organization</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>12</td><td colspan="2">Business Collaboration</td><td colspan="1">Capability Configuration</td><td colspan="1">Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">13</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="2">Business Event<br /><br /></td><td colspan="1">Operational Signal</td><td colspan="1"><p>Common Rule if has no relations. </p><p>The Send Signal Action is created additionally if the ArchiMate Business Event is related to Business Process. </p><p>Also, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/Mapping+issues">Mapping issues</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">State</td><td colspan="1"><p>The <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">ArchiMate Business Event is mapped to State i</span>f the ArchiMate Business Event is related to another Business Event and/or Junction using the Triggering relation. Additionally:</p><ul><li>Operational State Description is created if the ArchiMate Business Event is related to Resource. </li><li> Service State Description is created if ArchiMate Business Event is related to Business Function, which is related to Business Service. </li><li>Operational State Description is created if ArchiMate Business Event is related to Business Function, which is <strong>NOT</strong> related to Business Service. </li></ul></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="4">14</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="4">Business Function<br /><br /><br /><br /></td><td><p>Function</p></td><td>The ArchiMate Business Function is mapped to Function if the ArchiMate Business Function is <strong>NOT</strong> related to Business Service</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Function + Function Action</td><td colspan="1"><p>The ArchiMate Business Function is mapped to Function + Function Action if the ArchiMate Business Function is <strong>NOT</strong> related to Business Service and is related to an element that maps to UAF Resource Performer using one of the following:</p><ul><li>Assignment relation</li><li>Association relation</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Function</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Business Function is mapped to Service Function if the ArchiMate Business Function is related to Business Service.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Function + Service Function Action</td><td colspan="1"><p>The ArchiMate Business Function is mapped to Service Function + Service Function Action if the ArchiMate Business Function is related to Business Service and is related to Business Interface <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">using Association relation.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>15</td><td colspan="2">Business Interaction</td><td>CapabilityConfiguration</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>16</td><td colspan="2">Business Interface</td><td>Service Interface</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>17</td><td colspan="2">Business Object</td><td>Operational Information</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">18</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="2">Business Process<br /><br /></td><td>Operational Activity</td><td><p>The ArchiMate Business Process is mapped to Operational Activity if ArchiMate Business Process is <strong>NOT</strong> related to:</p><ul><li>Business Role using Assignment relation </li><li>Resource using Association relation </li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Operational Activity + Operational Activity Action</td><td colspan="1"><p>The ArchiMate Business Process is mapped to Operational Activity + Operational Activity Action if the ArchiMate Business Process is related to:</p><ul><li>Business Role using Assignment relation</li><li>Resource using Association relation </li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>19</td><td colspan="2">Business Role</td><td colspan="1">OperationalPerformer</td><td colspan="1">Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>20</td><td colspan="2">Business Service</td><td>Service</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>21</td><td colspan="2">Capability</td><td>Capability</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>22</td><td colspan="2">Communication Network</td><td colspan="1">Standard</td><td colspan="1">Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="5">23</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="5">Constraint<br /><br /><br /><br /><br /></td><td>Constraint</td><td><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="74a01555-399e-4250-8ab2-275178aa996f">ArchiMate Constraint is mapped to Constraint if no other ArchiMate Constraint import and mapping rule is applied</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Resource Constraint</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Constraint is mapped to Resource Constraint if the ArchiMate Constraint has any relation to the element which maps to UAF Resource Performer, Resource Information, Actual Resource, or Function.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Operational Constraint</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Constraint is mapped to Operational Constraint if the ArchiMate Constraint has any relation to the element which maps to UAF Operational Information, Operational Activity, Operational Exchange, Operational Performer, or Operational Signal.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Strategic Constraint</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Constraint is mapped to Strategic Constraint if the ArchiMate Constraint has any relation to the Capability element.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service Policy</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Constraint is mapped to Service Policy if the ArchiMate Constraint has any relation to the Business Service element.</td></tr><tr><td>24</td><td colspan="2">Contract</td><td>Resource Information</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>25</td><td colspan="2">Course Of Action</td><td colspan="1">Operational Activity</td><td colspan="1">Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>26</td><td colspan="2">Data Object</td><td>Resource Information</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>27</td><td colspan="2">Deliverable</td><td>Actual Project Milestone</td><td><p>Common Rule</p><p>Also, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/Mapping+issues">Mapping issues</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td>28</td><td colspan="2">Device</td><td colspan="1">Resource Artifact</td><td colspan="1">Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>29</td><td colspan="2">Distribution Network</td><td colspan="1">System</td><td colspan="1">Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">30</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="2">Driver</td><td><p>Measurement</p><p><br /></p></td><td>The ArchiMate Driver is mapped to Measurement for Property Set if the ArchiMate Driver element is related to the element which maps to UAF Property Set using the Association relation. </td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Measurement Set + Measurement</td><td colspan="1"><p>The ArchiMate Driver element map to Measurement Set with Measurement if the ArchiMate Driver element <strong>IS NOT</strong> related to the element which maps to UAF Property Set using association relation.</p><p><strong>Note</strong>. UAF Measurement Set and Measurement has the same name as the ArchiMate Driver element.</p></td></tr><tr><td>31</td><td colspan="2">Equipment</td><td>Resource Artifact</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>32</td><td colspan="2">Facility</td><td>Resource Artifact</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>33</td><td colspan="2">Gap</td><td>Risk</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>34</td><td colspan="2">Goal</td><td colspan="1">Enterprise Goal</td><td colspan="1">Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>35</td><td colspan="2">Grouping</td><td>Element Group </td><td><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Common Rule</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>Note</strong>. All ArchiMate elements related to the Grouping element after importing to UAF are added as Annotated Elements for Element Group [Comment] regardless of the relations' type (ArchiMate relations are not mapped to the real UAF relations).</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>36</td><td colspan="2">Implementation Event</td><td colspan="1">Actual Project Milestone</td><td colspan="1"><p>Common Rule</p><p>Also, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/Mapping+issues">Mapping issues</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="6">37</td><td rowspan="6"><p>Junction</p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></td><td rowspan="3">And Junction</td><td colspan="1">Fork Node</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Junction is mapped to Fork Node i<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="40056e74-6f67-4da9-9c5c-42e3b0dd1bfb">f the ArchiMate Junction after the import has 0 or 1 incoming control</ac:inline-comment-marker> flow and is related to Operational Activity + Operational Activity Action / Function + Function Action.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Join Node</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Junction is mapped to Join Node if the ArchiMate Junction after the import has <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5216d549-5ce9-40d7-a65f-aebc79a87fe4">&gt;1 incoming control</ac:inline-comment-marker> flow and is related to Operational Activity + Operational Activity Action / Function + Function Action.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Pseudostate</td><td colspan="1"><p>The ArchiMate Junction is mapped to Pseudostate if the ArchiMate Junction after the import is related to States and Transitions. The Pseudostate kind will be:</p><ul><li>Kind=Fork (if incoming &gt;1 transition)</li><li>Kind=Join (if incoming 0 or 1 transition)</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="3">Or Junction</td><td colspan="1">Decision Node</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Junction is mapped to Decision Node if the ArchiMate Junction after the import has <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="344e1ea3-bf87-4b7a-81b0-7482111b36e5">0 or 1 incoming control</ac:inline-comment-marker> flow and is related to Operational Activity + Operational Activity Action / Function + Function Action.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Merge Node</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Junction is mapped to Merge Node if the ArchiMate Junction after the import has <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c563d872-1a67-4bcc-a3e9-3588376f78bc">&gt;1 incoming control flow and is related to Operational Activity + Operational Activity Action / Function + Function Action.</ac:inline-comment-marker></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Pseudostate</td><td colspan="1"><p>The ArchiMate Junction is mapped to Pseudostate if the ArchiMate Junction after the import is related to States and Transitions. The Pseudostate kind will be:</p><ul><li>Kind=Junction (if incoming &gt;1 transition),</li><li>Kind=Choice (if incoming 0 or 1 transition). </li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>38</td><td colspan="2">Location</td><td>Actual Location</td><td><p>Common Rule</p><p>Also, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/Mapping+issues">Mapping issues</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td>39</td><td colspan="2">Material</td><td>Natural Resource</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>40</td><td colspan="2">Meaning</td><td>Concern</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>41</td><td colspan="2">Node</td><td>ResourceArtifact</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>42</td><td colspan="2">Note</td><td>Information</td><td><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Common Rule</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>Note</strong>. All ArchiMate elements are related to Note using Connection/Line. After import to UAF, related elements are added as Annotated Elements for the Information [Comment] element.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>43</td><td colspan="2">Outcome</td><td>Actual Measurement Set</td><td><p>Common Rule</p><p>Also, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/Mapping+issues">Mapping issues</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td>44</td><td colspan="2">Path</td><td>Protocol</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">45</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="2">Plateau</td><td><p>Capability Configuration</p></td><td><p>The ArchiMate Plateau is mapped to Capability Configuration if ArchiMate Plateau is <strong>NOT</strong> related to Goal using Realization relation.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Actual Enterprise Phase</td><td colspan="1"><p>The ArchiMate Plateau is mapped to Actual Enterprise Phase if the ArchiMate Plateau is related to Goal using Realization relation.</p><p>Also, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/Mapping+issues">Mapping issues</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td>46</td><td colspan="2">Principle</td><td>Requirement</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">47</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="2">Product</td><td><p>Resource Architecture</p></td><td>The ArchiMate Product is mapped to Resource Architecture if the ArchiMate Product is <strong>NOT</strong> related to Business Service.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Service</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Product is mapped to Service if ArchiMate Product is related to Business Service.</td></tr><tr><td>48</td><td colspan="2">Representation</td><td colspan="1">Resource Information</td><td colspan="1">Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>49</td><td colspan="2">Requirement</td><td>Requirement</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>50</td><td colspan="2">Resource</td><td>Operational Performer</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>51</td><td colspan="2">Stakeholder</td><td>Actual Organization</td><td><p>Common Rule</p><p>Also, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/Mapping+issues">Mapping issues</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td>52</td><td colspan="2">System Software</td><td>Software</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td>53</td><td colspan="2">Technology Collaboration</td><td colspan="1">Resource Architecture</td><td colspan="1">Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">54</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="2">Technology Event<br /><br /></td><td>Resource Signal</td><td><p>Common Rule is applied if the ArchiMate Technology Event has no relations. </p><p>The Send Signal Action is created additionally if the ArchiMate Technology Event is related to Resource Process or Resource Function.</p><p>Also, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/Mapping+issues">Mapping issues</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">State</td><td colspan="1">The Technology Event is mapped to State if the ArchiMate Technology Event is related to another Technology Event and/or Junction using Triggering relation. Additionally, the Resource State Description is created.</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">55</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="2">Technology Function<br /><br /></td><td>Function</td><td><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The ArchiMate Technology Function is mapped to Function if the ArchiMate Technology Function is <strong>NOT</strong> related to an element that maps to UAF Resource Performer using:</span></p><ul><li>Assignment relation</li><li>Association relation</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Function + Function Action</td><td colspan="1"><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The ArchiMate Technology Function is mapped to Function + Function Action if the ArchiMate Technology Function is related to </span>an element that maps to UAF Resource Performer using:</p><ul><li>Assignment relation</li><li>Association relation</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>56</td><td colspan="2">Technology Interaction</td><td>Resource Architecture</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">57</td><td colspan="2">Technology Interface</td><td colspan="1">Resource Interface</td><td colspan="1">Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">58</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="2">Technology Process<br /><br /></td><td>Function</td><td><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The ArchiMate Technology Process is mapped to Function if the ArchiMate Technology Process is <strong>NOT</strong> related to an element that maps to UAF Resource Performer using:</span></p><ul><li>Assignment relation</li><li>Association relation</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Function + Function Action</td><td colspan="1"><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The ArchiMate Technology Process is mapped to Function + Function Action if the ArchiMate Technology Process is related to </span>an element that maps to UAF Resource Performer using:</p><ul><li>Assignment relation</li><li>Association relation</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>59</td><td colspan="2">Technology Service</td><td>Resource Artifact</td><td><p>Common Rule</p><p>Also, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/Mapping+issues">Mapping issues</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">60</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="2">Value<br /><br /></td><td colspan="1"><p>Measurement</p><p><br /></p></td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Value element map to Measurement for Property Set if the ArchiMate Value element is related to the element which maps to UAF Property Set using Association relation. </td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Measurement Set + Measurement</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Value element map to Measurement Set with Measurement if the ArchiMate Value element <strong>IS NOT</strong> related to the element which maps to UAF Property Set using Association relation. (UAF Measurement Set and Measurement has the same name as the ArchiMate Value element).</td></tr><tr><td>61</td><td colspan="2">Value Stream</td><td>Operational Activity</td><td>Common Rule</td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2">62</td><td colspan="2" rowspan="2">Work Package<br /><br /></td><td colspan="1">ActualProject</td><td colspan="1"><p>Common Rule</p><p>Also, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/Mapping+issues">Mapping issues</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">ActualProject + ActualProjectMilestone</td><td colspan="1">The ArchiMate Work Package is mapped to ActualProject + ActualProjectMilestone if the ArchiMate Work Package is related to the ArchiMate Implementation Event or Deliverable using the Triggering relationship.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="15adf048-00bc-4269-a5d1-88ba2e2746a2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">195523620</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Relationship mapping" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Mapping issues" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00549: Enables

- page_id: `191928973`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928973/Enables
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T08:53:15.177+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A dependency relationship denoting that an Opportunity provides the means for achieving an Enterprise Goal or Objective.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

Dependency

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928974 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00550: Enhanced Security Control

- page_id: `191928974`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928974/Enhanced+Security+Control
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T08:53:06.158+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A type of Activity that specifies a safeguard or countermeasure prescribed for a Resource Performer. It is intended to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the Resource’s information and to meet a set of defined security requirements.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Activity

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">A type of Activity that specifies a safeguard or countermeasure prescribed for a Resource Performer. It is intended to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of the Resource’s information and to meet a set of defined security requirements. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Activity</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928975 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00551: Enhances

- page_id: `191928975`

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A dependency relationship relating the Enhanced Security Control to a Security Control.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Dependency

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928976 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00552: Enterprise Goal

- page_id: `191928976`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928976/Enterprise+Goal
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T08:52:41.967+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A statement about a state or condition of the enterprise to be brought about or sustained through appropriate Means. An Enterprise Goal amplifies an Enterprise Vision that is, it indicates what must be satisfied on a continuing basis to effectively attain the Enterprise Vision.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">A statement about a state or condition of the enterprise to be brought about or sustained through appropriate Means. An Enterprise Goal amplifies an Enterprise Vision that is, it indicates what must be satisfied on a continuing basis to effectively attain the Enterprise Vision.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Class</p><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00553: Enterprise Mission

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Data Type

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928981 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00557: Environment Kind

- page_id: `191928981`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928981/Environment+Kind
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T08:51:23.221+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of Environment. Its enumeration literals are:

- TerrainType - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind captures a kind of terrain used to describe the terrain state of an environment at a particular time (e.g., muddy, frozen ground, deep snow, etc.).
- WeatherConditions - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind captures a kind of weather condition (e.g., Typhoon, Hurricane, Very Hot, Humid, etc.).
- LightConditions - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind captures a kind of light condition (e.g., broad daylight, dusk, moonlit, etc.).
- CBRNEnvironment - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind is of a Chemical, Biological, Radiological, or Nuclear (CBRN) kind.
- SituationType - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind captures a kind of situation used to describe the types and levels of threat (e.g., Corrosive, Fire, Smoke, Peaceful, etc.).

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description </strong></p><p>Enumeration of the possible kinds of Environment. Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li>TerrainType - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind captures a kind of terrain used to describe the terrain state of an environment at a particular time (e.g., muddy, frozen ground, deep snow, etc.).</li><li>WeatherConditions - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind captures a kind of weather condition (e.g., Typhoon, Hurricane, Very Hot, Humid, etc.). </li><li>LightConditions - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind captures a kind of light condition (e.g., broad daylight, dusk, moonlit, etc.). </li><li>CBRNEnvironment - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind is of a Chemical, Biological, Radiological, or Nuclear (CBRN) kind. </li><li>SituationType - Indicates that the Environment associated with EnvironmentKind captures a kind of situation used to describe the types and levels of threat (e.g., Corrosive, Fire, Smoke, Peaceful, etc.).</li></ul><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p>UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A property of an Environment that is typed by a Condition. The kinds of Condition that can be represented are Location, GeoPoliticalExtentType and Environment.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Property

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928425 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00562: Exporting tables to Excel spreadsheets

- page_id: `191928425`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928425/Exporting+tables+to+Excel+spreadsheets
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-01-10T11:08:06.420+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Managing tables
- labels: ['export-tables-to-excel-spreadsheets']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can export an active table into the Excel, HTML or CSV formats.

To export a table

1. On the table toolbar, click the Export button.
2. In the Choose file dialog, specify its location, name, and type (*.xlsx, *.csv, *.html).
3. Click Save . The table is exported.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can export an active table into the Excel, HTML or CSV formats.</p><p>To export a table</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, click the <strong>Export</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Choose </strong><strong>file</strong> dialog, specify its location, name, and type (*.xlsx, *.csv, *.html).</li><li>Click <strong>Save</strong>.<br />The table is exported.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="sv-6_table_export_to_html.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Exporting tables to Excel spreadsheets" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926827 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00564: Filling in SOV-4a table

- page_id: `191926827`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926827/Filling+in+SOV-4a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T14:22:56.113+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint > MODAF. SOV-4a Service Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To fill in a SOV-4a table, do one of the following

- Add a new Service Policy by clicking the Add New button and select a constrained Service Interface. Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example, English or OCL). Click the intersection again and the relation will be deleted.
- Add an Existing Service Policy by clicking the Add Existing button and select a Service Policies.

- You can add or edit constrained elements for every row in the table.
- You can remove Rows (Service Policy) from the model or only from the table, order and export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on the cell to open more menus.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To fill in a SOV-4a table, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Service Policy by clicking the Add New button and select a constrained Service Interface. Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example, English or OCL). Click the intersection again and the relation will be deleted.</li><li>Add an Existing Service Policy by clicking the Add Existing button and select a Service Policies.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="046f2bc2-6273-43fd-b42b-7562bc642176"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can add or edit constrained elements for every row in the table.</li><li>You can remove Rows (Service Policy) from the model or only from the table, order and export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on the cell to open more menus.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

A dependency relationship that specifies a transition from one Asset, Standard, Competence to another future one. It is related to an Actual Enterprise Phase to give it a temporal context.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Dependency

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928285 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00567: Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog

- page_id: `191928285`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928285/Forecast+Kinds+in+Time+Periods+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:56:00.344+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs > Time Periods dialog
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Specified Dates

821278836

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Specified Dates

821278839

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Specified Dates

821278838

These set of instructions will explain how to choose**Forecast Kinds**and how to specify their values.

There are three types of Forecast Kind:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] - to divide the forecast into equal periods.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] - to divide the forecast into specific dates.
- **Specified** **Periods**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] - to divide the forecast into specific time periods.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

periodicTo specify Periodic Forecast Kind

1. Select **Periodic** as the **Forecast Kind.**
2. Specify From Date by doing one of the following:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='alt+down.png'] , or Alt+Down, and select an already existing date from the list.
  - Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select a date from the opened[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UPDMP183']**Date and Time Settings**dialog.
3. Specify forecast periods in the **Number of Periods** field.
4. Select **Length Unit:** Years, Months, or Days.
5. Specify **Period Length** of the forecast according to the **Length Unit.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An Example of a Periodic Forecast Kind.

All data defined in the**Time Periods** dialog is displayed automatically in columns for each period.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Periodic Forecast Kind in the Model

#### NOTE: Specified Dates

Specified Dates

For Specified Dates Forecast Kind, you only need to specify the Forecast Dates. The previous fields are uneditable.

specifieddatesTo specify Specified Dates Forecast Kind

1. Select Specified Dates as the Forecast Kind.
2. Under Forecast Dates, click [ATTACHMENT filename='three_dot_edit_small.png'] to open the Select ISO8601 Date Time dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_forecast_dates_property.png']
3. Select the existing ISO8601DateTime element (date) from the List. You can create a new one by taking the following steps.
  - In the **Select ISO8601 Date Time** dialog, make sure **Creation Mode** is on.
  - Select the appropriate package from the list in which you want to store a new **ISO8601DateTime** element (date).
  - Click the Create button. The new date element's specification window opens automatically. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_ISO8601_date_time_dialog.png']
  - In the Value field, click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] , and select an appropriate date from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Date and Time Settings dialog' space='UPDMP183']**Date and Time Settings** dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying_value_property_of_ISO8601DateTime.png']
  - Click Close, or create more dates by repeating steps c. and d.
4. Click**OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An Example of a Specified Dates Forecast Kind.

All data defined in the **Time Periods** dialog are displayed in columns in the table. In this case, three dates (2016-09-23, 2017-09-23, and 2018-03-23) were automatically divided into two columns (from 2016-09-23 to 2017-09-23, and from 2017-09-23 to 2018-03-23).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Specified Dates Forecast Kind in the Model

specifiedperiodsTo specify Specified Periods Forecast Kind

1. Select Specified Periods as the Forecast Kind.
2. In the Time Periods field, click [ATTACHMENT filename='three_dot_edit_small.png'] to open the Select Time Period dialog.
3. In the opened dialog, you can do the following:
  - Click the Tree [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2018-04-29 at 11.59.07 AM.png'] button and expand the tree until you find an existing Timeline element ( [ATTACHMENT filename='timeline_icon.png'] ) with existing [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and double-click on each one. Skip to Step 4.
  - Click the List [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2018-04-29 at 11.56.13 AM.png'] button and double-click each existing Time Periods ( [ATTACHMENT filename='time_periods_icon.png'] ). Skip to Step 4.
  - Create a new Timeline element with Time Periods . Here's how:
    - Ensure that element Creation Mode is turned on.
    - Select the appropriate package from the list in which you want to store a new Timeline element.
    - Click the Create button. The Specification window of the newly created Timeline opens automatically.
    - Type a name for the new Timeline, and click Close .
    - Select the newly created Timeline element.
    - Click the Create button, and select Time Period . The Specification window of the newly created Time Period opens automatically. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_time_periods.png']
    - Specify a Name, Start Date, and End Date. [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying_time_period_start_and_end_dates.png']
    - Click Close.
4. Click OK.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a Specified Periods Forecast Kind.

All time periods defined in the **Time Periods** dialog are displayed in the table as columns. In this case, three periods (2016 period, 2017 period, and 2018 period) with their specific start and end dates were defined and displayed as columns in the SV-9 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Specified Periods Forecast Kind in the Model

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="015320fb-3016-4e96-891f-a1212d84711c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">821278836</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bfeff590-adcf-416e-8a11-180aacd6f8a6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">821278839</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="830dc59e-8e17-48dd-8fd7-54fcfaedf1eb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">821278838</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">These set of instructions will explain how to choose </span><strong style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Forecast Kinds</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> and how to specify their values. </span></p><p>There are three types of Forecast Kind:</p><ul><li><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="periodic"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Periodic]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></strong> - <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">to divide the forecast into equal periods.</span></span></li><li><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="specifieddates"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specified Dates]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></strong> - to divide the forecast into specific dates.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:link ac:anchor="specifiedperiods"><ac:link-body><strong>Specified</strong> <strong>Periods</strong></ac:link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link> - <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">to divide the forecast into specific time periods.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title=" Three Forecast Kind property values in the Time Periods dialog: Periodic, Specified Dates, Specified Periods."><ri:attachment ri:filename="forecast_kinds_property.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f8a7d862-dcfd-483f-ad52-1d7da9e3f7eb"><ac:parameter ac:name="">periodic</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To specify Periodic Forecast Kind</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select <strong>Periodic</strong> as the <strong>Forecast Kind.</strong></span></span></li><li>Specify <strong>From Date</strong> by doing one of the following:<ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="alt+down.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">, or Alt+Down, and select an already existing date from the list.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span>Click </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="three_dot_edit_small.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span>, and select a date from the opened </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDMP183" ri:content-title="Date and Time Settings dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Date and Time Settings</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link><span> dialog.</span><br /></span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Specify forecast periods in the <strong>Number of Periods</strong> field.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="">Select <strong>Length Unit:</strong> Years, Months, or Days.</span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Specify <strong>Period Length</strong> of the forecast according to the <strong>Length Unit.</strong></span></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="When Forecast Kind property value is Periodic, in this case the periods of this forecast begin from 2016-09-23, it has three periods, the duration of each is one year. All specified data is displayed in the table as columns." ac:alt="When Forecast Kind property value is Periodic, in this case the periods of this forecast begin from 2016-09-23, it has three periods, the duration of each is one year. All specified data is displayed in the table as columns." ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="periodic_forecast_properties.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An Example of a Periodic Forecast Kind.</h6><p>All data defined in the<strong> Time Periods</strong> dialog is displayed automatically in columns for each period.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The forecast begins from 2016-09-23, it is divided into three periods with the duration of each one year and displayed as three columns in the SV-9 table after specifying data in the Time Periods dialog." ac:alt="The forecast begins from 2016-09-23, it is divided into three periods with the duration of each one year and displayed as three columns in the SV-9 table after specifying data in the Time Periods dialog."><ri:attachment ri:filename="periodic_forecast_kind_in_sv-9_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of Periodic Forecast Kind in the Model</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="63db4eb4-13c6-4716-9a8f-dcae28491ebd"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Specified Dates</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For Specified Dates Forecast Kind, you only need to specify the Forecast Dates. The previous fields are uneditable.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21ee8512-a2f2-4d2a-b0a1-336e16841f1f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">specifieddates</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To specify Specified Dates Forecast Kind</p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Specified Dates</strong> as the Forecast Kind.</li><li>Under <strong>Forecast Dates,</strong> click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="three_dot_edit_small.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to open the <strong>Select ISO8601 Date Time</strong> dialog.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The Edit button in the Forecast Dates property value box of the Time Periods dialog. One or more specific date(s) for the forecast can be defined in this property value box." ac:alt="The Edit button in the Forecast Dates property value box of the Time Periods dialog. One or more specific date(s) for the forecast can be defined in this property value box." ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_forecast_dates_property.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Select the existing <strong>ISO8601DateTime</strong> element (date) from the <strong>List.</strong> You can create a new one by taking the following steps. <br /><ul><li>In the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><strong>Select ISO8601 Date Time</strong> dialog, make sure <strong>Creation Mode</strong> is on.</span></li><li>Select the appropriate package from the list in which you want to store a new <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><strong>ISO8601DateTime</strong> element</span> (date).</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button. The new date element's specification window opens automatically.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="When creating a new date in the Select ISO8601 Date Time dialog, you need to select the package wherein you want to store the new date (b.), turn on the creation mode (a.), and click the Create button (c.)." ac:alt="When creating a new date in the Select ISO8601 Date Time dialog, you need to select the package wherein you want to store the new date (b.), turn on the creation mode (a.), and click the Create button (c.)."><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_ISO8601_date_time_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the <strong>Value</strong> field, click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, and select an appropriate date from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDMP183" ri:content-title="Date and Time Settings dialog" /><ac:link-body><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Date and Time Settings</strong></span></ac:link-body></ac:link> dialog.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="After the new ISO8601DateTime element is created, its specific date must be selected from the Date and Time Settings dialog which opens automatically when trying to edit the Value property box." ac:alt="After the new ISO8601DateTime element is created, its specific date must be selected from the Date and Time Settings dialog which opens automatically when trying to edit the Value property box."><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_value_property_of_ISO8601DateTime.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click </span><strong>Close,</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> or create more dates by repeating steps c. and d.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span>Click </span><strong>OK</strong><span>.</span></span></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="When the Forecast Kind property value is Specified Dates, in this case three specific dates of this forecast are defined: 2016-09-23, 2017-09-23, and 2018-03-23. These specific dates are displayed in the table as its columns."><ri:attachment ri:filename="specified_dates_forecast_kind.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>An Example of a Specified Dates Forecast Kind.</span></h6><p><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">All data defined in the <strong>Time Periods</strong> dialog are displayed in columns in the table. In this case, three dates (2016-09-23, 2017-09-23, and 2018-03-23) were automatically divided into two columns (from 2016-09-23 to 2017-09-23, and from 2017-09-23 to 2018-03-23).</span><br /></span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The forecast is divided into two periods and displayed as two columns in the the SV-9 table after specifying them in the Time Periods dialog." ac:alt="The forecast is divided into two periods and displayed as two columns in the the SV-9 table after specifying them in the Time Periods dialog."><ri:attachment ri:filename="specified_dates_forecast_kind_in_sv-9.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">Example of Specified Dates Forecast Kind in the Model</span></h6><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ffd486f3-8666-4982-8d0a-14d0c3958112"><ac:parameter ac:name="">specifiedperiods</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To specify Specified Periods Forecast Kind</p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Specified Periods</strong> as the Forecast Kind.</li><li>In the <strong>Time Periods</strong> field, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="three_dot_edit_small.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to open the <strong>Select Time Period</strong> dialog.</li><li>In the opened dialog, you can do the following:<ul><li>Click the <strong>Tree </strong><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2018-04-29 at 11.59.07 AM.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button and expand the tree until you find an existing Timeline element (<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="timeline_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>) <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">with existing <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="time_periods_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>,</span></span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">and double-click on each one. Skip to Step 4.</span></span></li><li>Click the <strong>List </strong><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2018-04-29 at 11.56.13 AM.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button and double-click each existing Time Periods (<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="time_periods_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>). Skip to Step 4.</li><li>Create a new Timeline element <strong>with</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Time Periods</span></span></span></span>. Here's how:<ul><li>Ensure that element <strong>Creation Mode</strong> is turned on.</li><li>Select the appropriate package from the list in which you want to store a new Timeline element.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button. The Specification window of the newly created Timeline opens automatically.</li><li>Type a name for the new Timeline, and click <strong>Close</strong>.</li><li>Select the newly created Timeline element.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button, and select <strong>Time Period</strong>. The Specification window of the newly created Time Period opens automatically.<ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_time_periods.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Specify a <strong>Name, Start Date,</strong> and <strong>End Date.</strong><br /><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The Specification window of the newly created Time Period with its Name, Start Date, and End Date properties defined." ac:alt="The Specification window of the newly created Time Period with its Name, Start Date, and End Date properties defined."><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_time_period_start_and_end_dates.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>Close.</strong></li></ul></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK.</strong></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="When the Forecast Kind property value is Specified Periods, in this case three time periods of forecast are defined. These periods are displayed in the Forecast table as its columns." ac:alt="When the Forecast Kind property value is Specified Periods, in this case three time periods of forecast are defined. These periods are displayed in the Forecast table as its columns."><ri:attachment ri:filename="specified_periods_forecast_properties.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of a Specified Periods Forecast Kind.</h6><p class="auto-cursor-target">All time periods defined in the <strong>Time Periods</strong> dialog are displayed in the table as columns. In this case, three periods (<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">2016 period, 2017 period, and 2018 period</span></span>) with their specific start and end dates were defined and displayed as columns in the SV-9 table.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The forecast is divided into three time periods and displayed as three columns in the the SV-9 table after specifying them in the Time Periods dialog. " ac:alt="The forecast is divided into three time periods and displayed as three columns in the the SV-9 table after specifying them in the Time Periods dialog. "><ri:attachment ri:filename="specified_periods_forecast_kind_in_sv-9_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">Example of Specified Periods Forecast Kind in the Model</span></h6><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

**Description**

An Activity which is specified in context of the Resource Performer (human or machine) that Is Capable Of Performing it.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Activity

**Description**

A call of a Function indicating that the Function is performed by a Resource Role in a specific context.

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Call Behavior Action

## PAGE 00573: Generating an Actual Measurements table from a Typical Measurements table

- page_id: `191928477`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928477/Generating+an+Actual+Measurements+table+from+a+Typical+Measurements+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-08-17T08:53:43.209+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Measurements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR2']Typical Measurements table can be instantiated by creating an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024xR2']Actual Measurementstable.

To generate anActual Measurements table from a Typical measurements table

1. Create a Typical Measurements table with at least one measurable Property Set added.
2. On the table toolbar, click the Evaluate Measurements button. A new Actual Measurements table is created.
3. Add actual values for all Actual Measurements . The Actual Property Set is created and the rows representing its slots defined by Measurements are added to the Actual Measurements table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span class="confluence-link">A </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Typical Measurements table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Typical Measurements table</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> can be instantiated by creating <span class="confluence-link">an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Actual Measurements table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body>Actual <span class="confluence-link">Measurements </span>table</ac:link-body></ac:link></span>.</p><p>To generate an<span class="confluence-link"> Actual Measurements table</span> from a <span class="confluence-link">Typical measurements table</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Typical Measurements table with at least one measurable<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Property Set </span>added.</li><li>On the table toolbar, click the <strong>Evaluate Measurements</strong> button. A new Actual Measurements table is created.</li><li class="confluence-link">Add <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">actual values for all Actual Measurements</span>.<br /><span>The Actual Property Set is created and the rows representing its slots defined by Measurements are added to the Actual Measurements table. </span></li></ol>
````

## PAGE 00574: Generating DARS report

- page_id: `191928359`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928359/Generating+DARS+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-10-09T04:49:55.111+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: ['dars-template', 'generate-dars-report-in-av-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before generating an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='AV-1 Overview and Summary Information' space='UAF12P2024xR2']([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DoDAF 2.0' space='UAF12P2024xR2']), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Summary and Overview' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.2' space='UAF12P2024xR2']) DARS report, you must load the DARS template in these diagrams and provide the template with relevant data.

To load the DARS template

1. Select the Architecture Description from the Containment tree and open its Specification window.
2. Click the Load DARS Template button to load DARS template. [ATTACHMENT filename='load_dars_template.png']
3. Fill in the extension property values for the DARS report. [ATTACHMENT filename='Architectural_description_specification_window.png']
4. Click **Close**.

To generate DARS report use standard [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating document based reports' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] procedure.

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before generating an A1 DARS report, you must load the DARS template in A1 diagram and provide the template with relevant data.

To load the DARS template in A1

1. Select the Architecture Description from the Containment tree and open its Specification window.
2. Click the Load DARS Template button to load DARS template. [ATTACHMENT filename='DARS_template.png']
3. Fill in the extension property values for the A1 DARS report. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To generate DARS report use standard [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating document based reports' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] procedure.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928414 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00576: Generating document based reports

- page_id: `191928414`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928414/Generating+document+based+reports
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-01-10T11:05:30.973+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Managing reports
- labels: ['create-report', 'quick-generate-report', 'report-wizard', 'generate-document-based-reports']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

216474442

216474445

216474441

**On this page**

216474444

The modeling tool report handling functionality allows you to store UAF report data in a UAF project. With this feature you can easily manage multiple reports with different properties and quickly print a report directly from the model. Another benefit from storing your report data within a project is that you can easily pass the report on to other users by passing the project. 
 UAFreports can be handled similarly as any other artifacts: diagrams, matrices, and tables. There is a report data element created by default in a project template for each UAF report. A report data element is filled in with predefined default data.

##### Creating a new report data element

You can create a new report data element from the shortcut menu of a viewpoint or view package. 
 
 To create a report data element

1. In the Containment tree, right-click a package for which you want create a report data element .
2. Choose **Generate Report**, select a required report group and a report template. A new report data element that stores the default data for a particular report is created.

The newly created report data element is filled in with default data for successful generation of the report. To see variables and data of the report, expand the report element node in the Containment tree.

##### Changing the data stored within a variable

If you need to change data stored within a variable, open the variable's Specification window and then edit the **Value** property value. 
 
 You can store not only variables as data within a report data element, but the editable default report properties as well. The following table lists available editable default report properties and their descriptions.

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Name | To specify the name of the report data element. |
| Generate Recursively | Set to true to generate the report, recursively including all inner packages within a defined report scope. |
| Data | To select one or more packages and / or elements to define a scope for generating the report. |
| Auto Image Size | To specify an image size in the report. |
| Empty Text | To specify a value to be inserted when data contains no value. |
| Image Format | To select an image format for all the images in the report. |
| Template | To select a report template.A report template is not a part of a project. |
| Variables | To add, remove, and order report input variables. |

To edit a report property, open the report data element's Specification window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Generating a report from the report data

You can generate a report directly from the report data.

To generate a report from the report data element

1. Right-click a report data element and click **Quick** **Generate** **Report**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Specify the location to save the generated report.
3. Select whether or not to open the generated report directly after the generation.

You can create several reports with different data for each type of the report.

##### Generating a report using the Report Wizard

You can also use the **Report** **Wizard** for report generation. Using this wizard, you can generate a report either from existing report data elements (after loading them to the wizard) or from external report data.

To open the **Report** **Wizard**

- Click **Tools** > **Report Wizard** and select the report templates for the UAF views.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00577: Geo Political Extent Type

- page_id: `191928994`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928994/Geo+Political+Extent+Type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T08:54:54.286+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A geospatial extent whose boundaries are defined by declaration or agreement by political parties.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

UML Data Type

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928995 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00578: Geo Political Extent Type Kind

- page_id: `191928995`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928995/Geo+Political+Extent+Type+Kind
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T08:54:43.580+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of GeoPoliticalExtentType. Its enumeration literals are:

- GeoFeatureType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of object that encompasses meteorological, geographic, and control features mission significance.
- RegionOfCountryType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of large, usually continuous segment of a political state, nation, or its territory.
- CountryType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of political state, nation, or its territory.
- RegionOfWorldType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of large, usually continuous segment of a surface or space; area.
- FacilityType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of a real property entity consisting of underlying land and one or more of the following: a building, a structure (including linear structures), a utility system, or pavement.
- SiteType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of Physical (geographic) location that is or was owned by, leased to, or otherwise possessed. Each site is assigned to a single installation. A site may exist in one of three forms: (1) Land only, where there are no facilities present and where the land consists of either a single land parcel or two or more contiguous land parcels. (2) Facility or facilities only, where the underlying land is neither owned nor controlled by the government. A stand-alone facility can be a site. If a facility is not a stand-alone facility, it must be assigned to a site. (3). Land and all the facilities thereon, where the land consists of either a single land parcel or two or more contiguous land parcels.
- InstallationType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of base, camp, post, station, yard, center, or other activity, including leased facilities, without regard to the duration of operational control. An installation may include one or more sites.
- OtherType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type not covered by the standard GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKinds.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description </strong></p><p>Enumeration of the possible kinds of GeoPoliticalExtentType. Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li>GeoFeatureType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of object that encompasses meteorological, geographic, and control features mission significance. </li><li>RegionOfCountryType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of large, usually continuous segment of a political state, nation, or its territory. </li><li>CountryType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of political state, nation, or its territory. </li><li>RegionOfWorldType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of large, usually continuous segment of a surface or space; area. </li><li>FacilityType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of a real property entity consisting of underlying land and one or more of the following: a building, a structure (including linear structures), a utility system, or pavement. </li><li>SiteType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of Physical (geographic) location that is or was owned by, leased to, or otherwise possessed. Each site is assigned to a single installation. A site may exist in one of three forms: (1) Land only, where there are no facilities present and where the land consists of either a single land parcel or two or more contiguous land parcels. (2) Facility or facilities only, where the underlying land is neither owned nor controlled by the government. A stand-alone facility can be a site. If a facility is not a stand-alone facility, it must be assigned to a site. (3). Land and all the facilities thereon, where the land consists of either a single land parcel or two or more contiguous land parcels. </li><li>InstallationType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type of base, camp, post, station, yard, center, or other activity, including leased facilities, without regard to the duration of operational control. An installation may include one or more sites. </li><li>OtherType - Indicates that the GeoPoliticalExtentType associated with the GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKind is a type not covered by the standard GeoPoliticalExtentTypeKinds.</li></ul><p><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p>UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926441 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00579: Getting started

- page_id: `191926441`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926441/Getting+started
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:52:52.082+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

UAF Plugin bundle includes SysML Plugin, SoaML Plugin, Cameo Requirements Modeler Plugin, and UAF Plugin. The product supports all DoDAF 1.5, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF 1.2, NAF, and NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views dependent on the selected user environment. Each user environment provides architecture framework specific concepts, artifacts, new project templates, samples, and architecture framework specific usability features. A user environment can be changed any time by fully converting model to meet requirements of the selected architecture framework.

#### NOTE: Important for the UPDM 2 users

Important for the UPDM 2 users

If you are working with UPDM 2, please see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation' space='UPDM2P2024xR2'] or open the PDF file from the modeling tool's main menu > **Help** > **Other Documentation** > **UPDM 2 Plugin UserGuide**.

More about getting started with the UAF plugin, see in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>UAF Plugin bundle includes SysML Plugin, SoaML Plugin, Cameo Requirements Modeler Plugin, and UAF Plugin. The product supports all DoDAF 1.5, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF 1.2, NAF, and NAF 4.0 viewpoints and views dependent on the selected user environment. Each user environment provides architecture framework specific concepts, artifacts, new project templates, samples, and architecture framework specific usability features. A user environment can be changed any time by fully converting model to meet requirements of the selected architecture framework.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e25f735-8f66-45f5-b299-867fbc4e0527"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important for the UPDM 2 users</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you are working with UPDM 2, please see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UPDM2P2024xR2" ri:content-title="UPDM 2 Plugin Documentation" /></ac:link> or open the PDF file from the modeling tool's main menu &gt; <strong>Help</strong> &gt; <strong>Other Documentation</strong> &gt; <strong>UPDM 2 Plugin UserGuide</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>More about getting started with the UAF plugin, see in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="71ae3a51-89ae-4af7-8008-67c3b5ec41db" /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928495 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00581: Handling inconsistencies between structure and behavior models

- page_id: `191928495`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928495/Handling+inconsistencies+between+structure+and+behavior+models
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-08T13:48:21.170+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > UAF Behavior to structure synchronization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tip

464453088

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tip

464453099

464453086

**On this page**

**3**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tip

464453089

Three validation rules complement each other to check whether the structure model is compatible with the behavior model. If any inconsistencies are found, the structure model can be automatically synchronized with the behavior model using solvers. First, you must synchronize interfaces to handle Connectors and, finally, Exchanges. As a consequence, manual work is reduced.

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

You can handle detected inconsistencies using solvers directly in:

- Internal Connectivity Diagram
- Validation Results panel

##### Handling missing Ports and Interfaces

The first validation rule checks whether Ports and Interfaces are compatible with flows between the Asset Roles. The missing Ports and Interfaces can be handled automatically using solvers.

To create missing Ports and Interfaces

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the Internal Connectivity Diagram, select an Asset Role highlighted in yellow, and then in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='triangle_symbol_smart_manipulator_toolbar.png'] . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the open **Validation Results** panel, right-click a validation result. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Select one of the following:

1. 
  - Create <viewpoint name> Port - creates a new Port typed by a compatible Interface.
  - Interface]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Handling inconsistencies between structure and behavior models' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] - changes an existing Port type to a compatible Interface.
  - Add Missing Flow Property - adds a new Flow Property to an Interface that types a Port.
  - Reverse Direction of <viewpoint name> Port - changes the Port direction, thus changing the Flow Property direction of an Interface that types a Port.

A Port typed by a compatible Interface is created.

###### Choosing a compatible Interface

If you select the **Choose Compatible Interface**solver, the **Choose Port with Compatible Interface**dialog opens.

You can now choose a Port and type it with an Interface with Flow Properties compatible with the type and direction of the missing flow.

Alternatively, you can create a new Port and an Interface to type the Port. For this, select **<NEW>**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Choosing a Port and specifying a compatible Interface as its type.

##### Handling missing Connectors

The second validation rule checks whether a Connector exists between the two compatible Ports owned by the Asset Role. The missing Connectors can be handled automatically using solvers.

To create missing Connectors

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the Internal Connectivity Diagram, select a Port highlighted in yellow, and then in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='triangle_symbol_smart_manipulator_toolbar.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='UAF_behavior_to_structure_connectors_validation_rule.png']
  - In the open Validation Results panel, right-click a validation result. [ATTACHMENT filename='UAF_behavior_to_structure_connectors_validation_rule_panel.png']
2. Select Create <viewpoint name> Connector .

A Connector between the two compatible Ports is created.

##### Handling missing Exchanges

The third validation rule checks realized Exchanges in Activities and help to ensure the correct Exchanges are realized on Connectors that connect Ports typed by compatible Interface. The missing Exchanges can be handled automatically using solvers.

To realize missing Exchanges on Connectors

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the Internal Connectivity Diagram, select a Connector highlighted in yellow, and then in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='triangle_symbol_smart_manipulator_toolbar.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='UAF_behavior_to_structure_exchanges_validation_rule.png']
  - In the open Validation Results panel, right-click a validation result. [ATTACHMENT filename='UAF_behavior_to_structure_exchanges_validation_rule_panel.png']
2. Select one of the following:
  - Realize Missing <viewpoint name> Exchange - realizes existing compatible Exchange found in the behavior model on the Connector.
  - Open <viewpoint name> Exchange Manager - opens the Exchange Manager* dialog .
  - Change Exchange Realization Compatibility Checking - opens the Project Options dialog to enable/disable the Exchange realization checking.

A compatible Exchange is realized on the selected Connector.

##### Navigating to elements

To learn more about the validated elements, in the smart manipulator toolbar of the Internal Connectivity Diagram or Validation Results panel, select the **Navigate To** command to navigate to:

- Activity Edges in the Process Flow diagram from which the missing flow has been identified.
- Asset Roles in the Internal Connectivity Diagram between which items are flowing.

Elements are automatically selected on the diagram pane and in the Containment tree.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Navigating to an element from the smart manipulator toolbar.

1851350132

**Related diagrams**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources Internal Connectivity diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Internal Connectivity (Resource) diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

*** Related Exchange Managers**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
