<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928997 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00582: High Level Operational Concept

- page_id: `191928997`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928997/High+Level+Operational+Concept
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T08:54:11.130+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Describes the Resources and Locations required to meet an operational scenario from an integrated systems point of view. It is used to communicate overall quantitative and qualitative system characteristics to stakeholders.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Class

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGBoldBulletKeepNextParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Describes the Resources and Locations required to meet an operational scenario from an integrated systems point of view. It is used to communicate overall quantitative and qualitative system characteristics to stakeholders. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Class</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929177 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00583: How to migrate

- page_id: `191929177`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929177/How+to+migrate
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:05.822+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before migrating your projects to the latest profile versions, we strongly advise you to make a backup first. After the migration is completed, you will not be able to reverse it.

For more information about migrating server projects, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migrating projects' space='MD2024xR2'].

.

If you agree to migrate the project, an auto-check of the project usage is performed. You are warned if there are used projects of incompatible versions that, you need to convert. Two types of usages can interrupt migration:

- Cyclic usage
- Indirect usage

If the project contains cyclic usage, you will receivea warning with those usages listed. To continue with the migration, you need to eliminate the listed cyclic usages first. Repeat the migration.

For information about how to identify cyclic usages, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying suspicious usages' space='TWCloud2024xR2']. The cyclic usages should be solved with the version you worked on before the migration.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After cyclic usages are eliminated, and you continue with the migration, the check for indirect usages will be performed.

If the main project contains indirectly used projects, you will receive a warning message listing those projects. To continue with the migration, you need to migrate the indirectly used projects one by one **in the same order they are listed in the message**. Repeat the migration.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After the cyclic and indirect usages are solved, you are informed about the directly used projects of incompatible versions. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud#ProjectmigrationissuesonTeamworkCloud-Usedprojectsaremigrated,butnotupdated)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

More about migrating UAF and UPDM projects:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF projects migration manual' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UPDM projects migration manual' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<div><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cee6011b-0cd8-4f2d-89d1-cd8d071c707f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Before migrating your projects to the latest profile versions, we strongly advise you to make a backup first. After the migration is completed, you will not be able to reverse it.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="015ab40b-16ae-4330-a8ed-746e3ac71725"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about migrating server projects, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Migrating projects" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><div>You are suggested to migrate each time you open a project created with the older version of UPDM / UAF with the modeling tool with the latest UAF version<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9e05a89f-f8c4-440f-b94f-84f299446569"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="167a9859-8516-48fb-a158-b784804c3e6c">.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><br /></div><div><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9e05a89f-f8c4-440f-b94f-84f299446569"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="167a9859-8516-48fb-a158-b784804c3e6c"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="migration_question_new.png.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="How to migrate" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></div><div><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">If you agree to migrate the project, an auto-check of the project usage is performed. You are warned if there are used projects of incompatible versions that, you need to convert. Two types of usages can interrupt migration:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Cyclic usage</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Indirect usage</span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">If the project contains cyclic usage, you will receive </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">a warning with those usages listed. To continue with the migration, you need to eliminate the listed cyclic usages first. Repeat the migration.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4eeac4a9-7b3f-4916-b856-df9bd9a159ec"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For information about how to identify cyclic usages, see <ac:link ac:anchor="Cyclic Usages"><ri:page ri:space-key="TWCloud2024xR2" ri:content-title="Identifying suspicious usages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cyclic Usages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The cyclic usages should be solved with the version you worked on before the migration.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="warning cycle.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="How to migrate" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>After cyclic usages are eliminated, and you continue with the migration, the check for indirect usages will be performed.</p><p>If the main project contains indirectly used projects, you will receive a warning message listing those projects. To continue with the migration, you need to migrate the indirectly used projects one by one <u><strong>in the same order they are listed in the message</strong></u>. Repeat the migration.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="question2.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="How to migrate" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>After the cyclic and indirect usages are solved, you are informed about the directly used projects of incompatible versions. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/UAF12P2024xR2/Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud#ProjectmigrationissuesonTeamworkCloud-Usedprojectsaremigrated,butnotupdated">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="263" ac:width="807"><ri:attachment ri:filename="pic2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="How to migrate" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>  </p><div><p><br /></p></div><div><p class="auto-cursor-target">More about migrating UAF and UPDM projects:</p></div><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UAF projects migration manual" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UPDM projects migration manual" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00585: Impact analysis relation maps

- page_id: `191928435`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928435/Impact+analysis+relation+maps
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-01-10T11:12:25.211+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Managing traceability and impact analysis
- labels: ['impact-analysis-relation-maps', 'node-role-impact-analysis-map', 'node-impact-analysis-map', 'resource-role-impact-analysis-map', 'resource-impact-analysis-map']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

216907718

216907721

216907719

Take your model to the next steps - perform the impact analysis. For this purpose, there are four predefined relation maps. Using these maps, you are able to identify the model elements which are affected by the changes of the context element.

The predefined relation maps for the impact analysis are the following:

- Operational Role Impact Analysis Map
- Operational Performer Impact Analysis Map
- Resource Role Impact Analysis Map
- Resource Impact Analysis Map

Several predefined criteria helps you to determine how the context element is related to other model elements.

The Legend in the right-top corner shows you which color represents which criteria.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

216907717

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating predefined relation map' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modifying criteria through the legend' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

## PAGE 00586: Impact Matrix

- page_id: `191928363`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928363/Impact+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:01.768+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1335007736

1335007764

1335007753

###### Description

The Impact Matrix describes the mapping between the Strategic/Operational/Resource/Service Elements and Strategic Elements using the Impacted By relationship directly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2'].

###### Implementation

The rows of the matrix are:

- Operational Elements (Operational Activity and Operational Architecture)
- Resources Elements (Resource Architecture)
- Services Elements (Service Architecture)
- Strategic Elements (Actual Outcome, Capability, and Actual Strategic Phase)

The columns of the matrix are:

- Strategic Elements (Challenge, Opportunity, and Enterprise Goal)

###### Sample

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

## PAGE 00588: Implementation Matrix

- page_id: `191928366`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928366/Implementation+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:01.803+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: ['implementation-matrix', 'mapping-between-systems-operational-and-service-elements', 'nodes/performers-implementation-matrix', 'operational-activities-implementation-matrix', 'operational-exchanges-implementation-matrix', 'ov-sv-gap-analysis']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1370353206

1370353209

1370353207

**Description**

The **Implementation Matrix** describes the mapping between the Resources / Operational / Summary & Overview Elements and Operational / Services / Strategic / Resources Elementsdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2'].The implementation in UAF is defined by the Implements relationship. To specify the implementation and analyze implementation gaps, the Implementation Matrix is added as a supportive product for UAF.

**Implementation**

An Implementation Matrix is represented by a Dependency Matrix-based diagram.

The Rows of the matrix are:

- Resources Elements: Resource Performer, Function, Resource Interfaces, Resource Information, Resource Role, Resource Service, Resource Connector, and Resource Exchange.
- Operational Elements: Operational Activity and Operational Information.
- Service Elements: Service Interface.
- Summary & Overview Elements: Architecture.

The Columns of this matrix are:

- Operational Elements: Operational Agent, Operational Activity, Operational Interface, Operational Information, Operational Role, Operational Connector, and Operational Exchange.
- Services Elements: Service, Service Function, and Service Interface.
- Strategic Elements: Actual Strategic Phase and Strategic Information.
- Resource Elements: Resource Connector.

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

**Predefined configurations**

The predefined matrices are only available in DoDAF and DoDAF 2.0 perspectives.

Working in DoDAF or DoDAF 2.0 perspectives, you can find predefined matrices by clicking **Analyze** > **OV-SV Gap analysis**.

There are several types of predefined implementation matrices:

- Operational Performers Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Performers to System Resources only.
- Operational Activities Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Activities to Functions.
- Operational Exchanges Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Exchanges to Resource Exchanges.

These matrices provide an analysis of the whole model of a particular implementation, so you do not have to define the scope or any additional properties for building them.

1370353205

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='System' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Performer' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024xR2']

Overview Elements: Architecture.</span></span></li></ul><p>The Columns of this matrix are:</p><ul><li>Operational Elements: Operational Agent, Operational Activity, Operational Interface, Operational Information, Operational Role, Operational Connector, and Operational Exchange.</li><li>Services Elements: Service, Service Function, and Service Interface.</li><li>Strategic Elements: Actual Strategic Phase and Strategic Information.</li><li>Resource Elements: Resource Connector.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:title="Implementation Matrix" ac:alt="Implementation Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Implementation_matrix.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Implementation Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></strong></p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Predefined configurations</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45c0cb87-2f2d-4406-96ab-d5a253e8e5e5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The predefined matrices are only available in DoDAF and DoDAF 2.0 perspectives.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Working in DoDAF or DoDAF 2.0 perspectives, you can find predefined matrices by clicking <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>OV-SV Gap analysis</strong>.</p><p>There are several types of predefined implementation matrices:</p><ul><li>Operational Performers Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Performers to System Resources only.</li><li>Operational Activities Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Activities to Functions.</li><li>Operational Exchanges Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Exchanges to Resource Exchanges.</li></ul><p>These matrices provide an analysis of the whole model of a particular implementation, so you do not have to define the scope or any additional properties for building them.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32508abf-c16f-449c-a106-1b7e23db9cd6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1370353205</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="System" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Function" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Performer" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Activity" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="100aa383-ba7c-4685-b1d3-2b06ffa80227" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00589: Implements

- page_id: `191928999`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928999/Implements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T10:35:46.794+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An Abstraction relationship that defines how an element in the upper layer of abstraction is implemented by a semantically equivalent element (i.e. tracing the OperationalActivities to the Functions that implement them) in the lower level of abstraction.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extensions**

UML Abstraction

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGBoldBulletKeepNextParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">An Abstraction relationship that defines how an element in the upper layer of abstraction is implemented by a semantically equivalent element (i.e. tracing the OperationalActivities to the Functions that implement them) in the lower level of abstraction. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong><span class="fontstyle0">Extensions</span></strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span class="fontstyle2">UML Abstraction</span></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928369 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00590: Importing from ArchiMate

- page_id: `191928369`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928369/Importing+from+ArchiMate
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-11-03T13:00:33.141+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Introduction to ArchiMate

The ArchiMate language is an open and independent modeling language for Enterprise Architecture that enables Enterprise Architects to describe, analyze, and visualize the relationships among architecture domains in an unambiguous way. The ArchiMate supports an exchange format in XML, which allows model and diagram exchange between tools.

https://publications.opengroup.org/standards/archimate

##### Importing ArchiMate model from open exchange file

You can import ArchiMate model to your UAF project. The supported file format for importing is ArchiMate *.xml*.

To import the model from ArchiMate .xml file

1. In the main menu, click File > Import From > ArchiMate XML File .
2. In the open dialog, choose the ArchiMate .xml file you want to import.
3. Click Open .
4. In the Select Owner dialog, choose or create the owner and click OK .

After the import is completed, the notification is displayed with the import results. The imported ArchiMate elements are converted to the UAF elements and added to your model. You can find the imported elements in the Containment tree. How ArchiMate model elements and relationships map to the UAF elements described in the following pages:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element mapping' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Relationship mapping' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

Also, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Mapping issues' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] chapter to learn about known mapping issues.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Introduction to ArchiMate</h3><p>The ArchiMate language is an open and independent modeling language for Enterprise Architecture that enables Enterprise Architects to describe, analyze, and visualize the relationships among architecture domains in an unambiguous way. The ArchiMate supports an exchange format in XML, which allows model and diagram exchange between tools.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e997923-3556-47be-aa3e-91a4a374d395"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information, see <a href="https://publications.opengroup.org/standards/archimate">https://publications.opengroup.org/standards/archimate</a>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Importing ArchiMate model from open exchange file</h3><p>You can import ArchiMate model to your UAF project. The supported file format for importing is ArchiMate <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.xml</em>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To import the model from ArchiMate .xml file</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>ArchiMate XML File</strong>.</li><li>In the open dialog, choose the ArchiMate<em> .xml</em> file you want to import.</li><li>Click <strong>Open</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Owner</strong> dialog, choose or create the owner and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>After the import is completed, the notification is displayed with the import results. The imported ArchiMate elements are converted to the UAF elements and added to your model. You can find the imported elements in the Containment tree. How ArchiMate model elements and relationships map to the UAF elements described in the following pages:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Element mapping" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Relationship mapping" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul><p>Also, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Mapping issues" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link> chapter to learn about known mapping issues.</p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1119"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ArchiMate 3.1 to UAF 1.2 horizontal-white-background.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="Importing from ArchiMate" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="625"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2022-11-3_14-59-16.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Importing from ArchiMate" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929000 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00592: Information

- page_id: `191929000`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929000/Information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T10:35:34.876+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A comment that describes the state of an item of interest in any medium or form -- and is communicated or received.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Comment

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929001 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00593: Information Model

- page_id: `191929001`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929001/Information+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T10:35:21.358+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A structural specification of information types, showing relationships between them. The type of information captured in the Information Model is described using the enumeration Information Model Kind (Conceptual,Logical and Physical).

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Package

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A structural specification of information types, showing relationships between them. The type of information captured in the Information Model is described using the enumeration Information Model Kind (Conceptual,Logical and Physical).</p><p><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><strong>Extension</strong></p><p>Package</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929002 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00594: Information Model Kind

- page_id: `191929002`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929002/Information+Model+Kind
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T10:35:07.078+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Information Model Kind is an enumeration of the possible kinds of Data Model. Its enumeration literals are:

- Conceptual - Indicates that the Data Model associated with the Data Model Kind is a conceptual Data Model that defines the required high-level data concepts and their relationships.
- Logical - Indicates that the Data Model associated with the Data Model Kind is a logical data model that allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation specific or product specific issues. It details the conceptual data model.
- Physical - Indicates that the Data Model associated with the Data Model Kind is a physical data model that is an implementable specification of a data structure. A physical data model realizes a logical data model, taking into account implementation restrictions and performance issues while still enforcing the constraints, relationships and typing of the logical data model.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Enumeration

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">The Information Model Kind is an enumeration of the possible kinds of Data Model. Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li>Conceptual - Indicates that the Data Model associated with the Data Model Kind is a conceptual Data Model that defines the required high-level data concepts and their relationships.</li><li>Logical - Indicates that the Data Model associated with the Data Model Kind is a logical data model that allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation specific or product specific issues. It details the conceptual data model.</li><li>Physical - Indicates that the Data Model associated with the Data Model Kind is a physical data model that is an implementable specification of a data structure. A physical data model realizes a logical data model, taking into account implementation restrictions and performance issues while still enforcing the constraints, relationships and typing of the logical data model.</li></ul><p><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p><strong><span class="fontstyle2">Extension</span></strong></p><p>Enumeration</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928553 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00596: Inheritance, Aggregation, Composition, and Redefinition of Exchanges

- page_id: `191928553`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928553/Inheritance+Aggregation+Composition+and+Redefinition+of+Exchanges
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-05-17T15:46:39.588+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Working with exchanges
- labels: ['inheritance-of-exchanges', 'aggregation-of-exchanges', 'composition-of-exchanges', 'redefinition-of-exchanges', 'aggregated-information', 'display-all-operational-exchanges', 'hide-inherited-exchanges', 'see-inherited-exchanges', 'redefine-operational-exchange']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational/Resource/Service Exchanges can be inherited, aggregated, or composed. You can redefine the inherited flows.

You can see the inherited, aggregated, and composed exchanges in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] dialog
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] dialog
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] dialog
- Operational/Resources/Services/Personnel/Security (Operational and Resource) Role-based Connectivity tables.

The simplified scenarios illustrating the Aggregation and Inherited Exchanges are described in the following cases:

###### Case 1. Aggregated Exchanges

Let's say, we have Operational Performers *Helicopter*and *Signal Receiver* related to the Operational Exchange *Reported Condition*. Then we draw an association between *Medical Assistance* and *Signal Receiver*. In this case, the drawn association can realize the *Reported Condition* exchange, since the Operational Performer *Medical Assistance* aggregates the Operational Performer *Helicopter*.

If Composition is used instead of Aggregation to connect Medical Assistance and Helicopter, the Exchanges will be collected the same way as in the Aggregation case.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To hide aggregated exchanges in dialogs

1. Click Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, select General > UAF .
3. Set the Respect UAF Information Flows on Aggregation property value to false .

- The exchange is not created for the drawn association, it is only realized.
- The realization of the exchange will be removed from the association when Aggregation or Exchange is removed.

All the aggregated exchanges can also be seen in the role-based connectivity tables. The aggregated exchange rows are displayed in gray.

To see the aggregated exchanges

1. On the table toolbar, select Show Rows.
2. In the open menu, click the Aggregated Operational Exchange or Resource **Exchange** command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Case 2. Inherited Exchanges

Let's say, we have an Operational Performers *Search**Node* and *Place Of Safety* related to the Operational Exchange *Warning Order*. The Operational Performer *Aircraft* specifies the Operational Performer *Search**Node*. Then we draw an association between *Aircraft* and *Place O**f**Safety*. In this case, the drawn association inherits the Operational Exchange information that can be realized and redefined.

Let's realize the Operational Exchange. We can do this in two ways:

- By clicking [ATTACHMENT filename='display_all_operational_exchanges.png'] in the association smart manipulator toolbar.
- By selecting the check box in the Realize column of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Operational****Exchange****Manager** dialog .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To hide inherited exchanges in dialogs

1. Click Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, select General > UAF .
3. Set the Respect UAF Information Flows on Generalization property value to false .

- The exchange is not created for the drawn association, it is only realized.
- The realization of the exchange will be removed from the association when Generalization or Exchange is removed.

All the inherited exchanges can also be seen in the role-based connectivity tables. The inherited exchange rows are displayed in gray.

To see the inherited exchanges

1. On the table toolbar, select Show Rows.
2. In the open menu, click the Inherited Operational Exchange or Resource **Exchange** command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

When we have the inherited Operational Exchange, we can redefine it. This means, that the inherited Operational Exchange will be copied together with the information such as Actual Measurements, Conveyed Items, Documentation. The new Operational Exchange will be created in the model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To redefine the Operational Exchange

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Operational****Exchange****Manager** dialog , select the inherited Operational Exchange and click the Redefine button. The new Operational Exchange is created.

All the information that comes from the inherited exchange when redefining it can be modified in the model.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Strategic Exchange wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Operational/Resource/Service Exchanges can be inherited, aggregated, or composed. You can redefine the inherited flows.</p><p>You can see the inherited, aggregated, and composed exchanges in:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Exchange Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> dialog</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resource Exchange Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> dialog</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Service Exchange Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> dialog</li><li>Operational/Resources/Services/Personnel/Security (Operational and Resource) Role-based Connectivity tables.<span> </span></li></ul><p><span>The simplified scenarios illustrating the Aggregation and Inherited Exchanges are described in the following cases:</span></p><p><span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dba0c654-686a-4b34-b644-0ce46be559ac" /></span></p><h4><span>Case 1. Aggregated Exchanges</span></h4><p><span>Let's say, we have Operational Performers <em>Helicopter </em>and <em>Signal Receiver</em> related to the Operational Exchange <em>Reported Condition</em>. Then we draw an association between <em>Medical Assistance</em> and <em>Signal Receiver</em>. In this case, the drawn association can realize the <em>Reported Condition</em> exchange, since the Operational Performer <em>Medical Assistance</em> aggregates the Operational Performer <em>Helicopter</em>.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b773f29c-06f7-438b-abbb-e7df6ba917c0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If Composition is used instead of Aggregation to connect Medical Assistance and Helicopter, the Exchanges will be collected the same way as in the Aggregation case.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span><ac:image ac:title="Aggregation of information flow" ac:alt="Aggregation of information flow"><ri:attachment ri:filename="aggregated_information.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Inheritance, Aggregation, Composition, and Redefinition of Exchanges" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cc237fa9-641c-4802-a43e-fcc177c0b73f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To hide aggregated exchanges in dialogs</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Options </strong>&gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the<strong> Project Options</strong> dialog, select <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>UAF</strong>.</li><li>Set the <strong>Respect</strong><strong> UAF </strong><strong>Information</strong><strong> Flows </strong><strong>on</strong><strong> Aggregation</strong> property value to <em>false</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7614ef55-b73c-44c6-ba9a-bda57482510b"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The exchange is not created for the drawn association, it is only realized.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The realization of the exchange will be removed from the association when Aggregation or Exchange is removed.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>All the aggregated exchanges can also be seen in the role-based connectivity tables. The aggregated exchange rows are displayed in gray. </p><p><br /></p><p>To see the aggregated exchanges</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, select<strong> Show</strong><strong> Rows.</strong></li><li>In the open menu, click the <strong>Aggregated Operational</strong><strong> Exchange</strong> or <strong>Resource</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong> Exchange </strong></span>command.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="see_aggregated.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Inheritance, Aggregation, Composition, and Redefinition of Exchanges" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4>Case 2. Inherited Exchanges</h4><p>Let's say, we have an Operational Performers <em>Search </em><em>Node</em> and <em>Place Of Safety</em> related to the Operational Exchange <em>Warning Order</em>. The Operational Performer <em>Aircraft</em> specifies the Operational Performer <em>Search</em><em> Node</em>. Then we draw an association between <em>Aircraft</em> and <em>Place O</em><em>f</em><em> Safety</em>. In this case, the drawn association inherits the Operational Exchange information that can be realized and redefined.</p><p>Let's realize the Operational Exchange. We can do this in two ways:</p><ul><li>By clicking <ac:image ac:title="Display All Operational Exchanges" ac:alt="Display All Operational Exchanges"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_operational_exchanges.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the association smart manipulator toolbar.</li><li>By selecting the check box in the Realize column of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational</strong><strong> Exchange</strong><strong> Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="inheritance_in_operational_exchange_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Inheritance, Aggregation, Composition, and Redefinition of Exchanges" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e872cb39-c48c-491e-9d0e-92fee41834fa"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To hide inherited exchanges in dialogs</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Options </strong>&gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the<strong> Project Options</strong> dialog, select <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>UAF</strong>.</li><li>Set the <strong>Respect</strong><strong> UAF </strong><strong>Information</strong><strong> Flows </strong><strong>on</strong><strong> Generalization</strong> property value to <em>false</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06847455-5bf0-4a08-bbb6-30cd3273d138"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The exchange is not created for the drawn association, it is only realized.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The realization of the exchange will be removed from the association when Generalization or Exchange is removed.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />All the inherited exchanges can also be seen in the role-based connectivity tables. The inherited exchange rows are displayed in gray. </p><p><br /></p><p>To see the inherited exchanges</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, select<strong> Show</strong><strong> Rows.</strong></li><li>In the open menu, click the <strong>Inherited</strong><strong> Operational</strong><strong> Exchange</strong> or <strong>Resource</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong> Exchange </strong></span>command.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="inheritance_role_based.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Inheritance, Aggregation, Composition, and Redefinition of Exchanges" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>When we have the inherited Operational Exchange, we can redefine it. This means, that the inherited Operational Exchange will be copied together with the information such as Actual Measurements, Conveyed Items, Documentation. The new Operational Exchange will be created in the model.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The redefinition of Operational Exchange Warning Order" ac:alt="The redefinition of Operational Exchange Warning Order"><ri:attachment ri:filename="redefinition_of_inheritance.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Inheritance, Aggregation, Composition, and Redefinition of Exchanges" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br /></p><p>To redefine the Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational</strong><strong> Exchange</strong><strong> Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, select the inherited Operational Exchange and click the <strong>Redefine</strong> button. The new Operational Exchange is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d8eb23ae-ce99-4912-b51e-a121df164715"><ac:rich-text-body><p>All the information that comes from the inherited exchange when redefining it can be modified in the model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with exchanges" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Strategic Exchange wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928403 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00597: Instantiating structures

- page_id: `191928403`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928403/Instantiating+structures
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-01-10T10:40:19.589+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: ['automatic-instantiation-wizard', 'create-instance', 'instantiate-proposed-typical-structure', 'instant-structures', 'typical-uaf-element', 'actual-uaf-element']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automatic Instantiation wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Automatic Instantiation** wizard to instantiate any typical structure of UAF architecture automatically.

To open the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select any number of instantiable UAF elements.
2. Right-click them and choose Tools > Create Instance . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automatic Instantiation wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Automatic Instantiation** wizard opens.

To instantiate the proposed typical structure

1. Select all typical classes you want to instantiate in a diagram pane or in the Model Browser. [ATTACHMENT filename='ov_4.png']
2. Right-click them and select **Tools** > **Create****Instance**.
3. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automatic Instantiation wizard' space='UAF12P2024xR2']**Automatic Instantiation** wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done. A new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] Actual diagram is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For instantiable UAF elements corresponding actual elements are created. For example, by instantiating Organization results in Actual Organization and etc. For all other structural elements UML Instance Specifications are created.

| Typical UAF element | Actual UAF element |
| --- | --- |
| Project | Actual Project |
| Project Milestone | Actual Project Milestone |
| Project Theme | Project Status |
| Project Milestone Role | Actual Project Milestone Role |
| Organization | Actual Organization |
| Sub Organization (Resource Role typed by and owned by Organization) | Actual Organization Role |
| Post | Actual Post |
| Post Role (Resource Role typed by Post and owned by Organization) | Actual Organization Role |
| Person | Actual Person |
| Measurement Set | Actual Measurement Set |
| Measurement | Actual Measurement |
| Location | Actual Location |
| Capability Configuration | Fielded Capability |
| Environment | Actual Environment |
| Condition | Actual Condition |
| Resource Performer | Actual Resource |
| Enterprise Phase | Actual Enterprise Phase |
| Enduring Task | Actual Enduring Task |
| Responsibility | Actual Responsibility |
| Risk | Actual Risk |
| Natural Resource | Actual Resource |
| Resource Architecture | Actual Resource |
| Resource Artifact | Actual Resource |
| Software | Actual Resource |
| Technology | Actual Resource |
| System | Actual Resource |
| Resource Mitigation | Actual Resource |
| Security Enclave | Actual Resource |
| Known Resource | Actual Resource |
| Operational Performer | Actual Property Set |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Use<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Automatic Instantiation wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> to instantiate any typical structure of UAF architecture automatically.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To open the <span class="confluence-link"><strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Select any number of instantiable UAF elements.</li><li>Right-click them and choose <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Instance</strong>.<br />The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Automatic Instantiation wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li></ol><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To instantiate the proposed typical structure</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Select all typical classes you want to instantiate in a diagram pane or in the Model Browser.</p><ac:image ac:title="OV-4 Typical" ac:alt="OV-4 Typical"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ov_4.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Instantiating structures" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>Right-click them and select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Create</strong><strong> Instance</strong>.<span> </span></p></li><li><p>Follow the steps of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Automatic Instantiation wizard" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:link-body><strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />A new <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> Actual diagram is created.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="OV-4 Actual" ac:alt="OV-4 Actual"><ri:attachment ri:filename="OV-4_actual.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Instantiating structures" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>For instantiable UAF elements corresponding actual elements are created. For example, by instantiating Organization results in Actual Organization and etc. For all other structural elements UML Instance Specifications are created.</p><p> </p><table><colgroup><col /><col <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928407 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
````
## PAGE 00598: Integration with the Concept Modeler Plugin

- page_id: `191928407`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928407/Integration+with+the+Concept+Modeler+Plugin
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:02.165+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Concept Modeler Plugin (CCM) is integrated into the Cameo Enterprise Architecture, UPDM2, and UAF plugins. This plugin replaces the Soa+ Plugin. 
You can make the ontology modeling available in the UAF projects by creating the **Concept Modeling Diagram** from the **Create Diagram** dialog. After the diagram is created, the CCM profiles are loaded and CCM features are available in the project.

For more information about how to work with the Concept Modeler plugin, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Concept Modeler Documentation' space='CCM2024xR2'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Concept Modeler Plugin (CCM) is integrated into the Cameo Enterprise Architecture, UPDM2, and UAF plugins. This plugin replaces the Soa+ Plugin.<br />You can make the ontology modeling available in the UAF projects by creating the <strong>Concept Modeling Diagram</strong> from the <strong>Create Diagram</strong> dialog. After the diagram is created, the CCM profiles are loaded and CCM features are available in the project.</p><p>For more information about how to work with the Concept Modeler plugin, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CCM2024xR2" ri:content-title="Concept Modeler Documentation" /></ac:link>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928408 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00599: Internal diagrams context

- page_id: `191928408`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928408/Internal+diagrams+context
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:42:02.211+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating a new internal diagram for the element which cannot be the context of this diagram (e.g. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MD2024xR2']), the new context element is created automatically.

This feature is available for all UAF diagrams based on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MD2024xR2'].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure Diagram context' space='MD2024xR2']true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When creating a new <span class="ancestor-link parent-link">internal diagram</span> for the element which cannot be the context of this diagram (e.g. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Package" /></ac:link>), the new context element is created automatically.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e38c6b0b-6a9d-4046-9866-ebce84926bb4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This feature is available for all UAF diagrams based on <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Composite Structure Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51264c2b-6f0d-46cc-b252-aeb25017b07d"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Composite Structure Diagram context" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p>
````

- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929003/Is+Capable+To+Perform

- version_date: `2023-10-10T10:34:56.475+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Is Capable To Perform relationship defines the traceability between the Capable Elements to the Activities that they can perform.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Abstraction

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


## PAGE 00601: ISO8601 Date Time

- page_id: `191929004`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929004/ISO8601+Date+Time
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T10:34:43.443+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A date and time specified in the ISO8601 date-time format including timezone designator (TZD): YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssTZD.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

Literal String

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">A date and time specified in the ISO8601 date-time format including timezone designator (TZD): YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssTZD. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">Literal String</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929286 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00602: Issues related to collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `191929286`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929286/Issues+related+to+collaboration+powered+by+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-01-28T08:12:52.538+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are several known issues when working with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform:

- Migration from UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2 is not available.
- The Change Library Version action is unavailable for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using libraries' space='']
- Migration of UPDM 2.1 project containing UJTL or DISR libraries from version 2022x to 2024x (upgrading profiles) is not available on Power'By.

For more information about working with the platform, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='MD2024xR2'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There are several known issues when working with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Migration from UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2 is not available.</li><li>The <strong>Change Library Version</strong> action is unavailable for <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using libraries" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UJTL and DISR libraries.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Migration of UPDM 2.1 project containing UJTL or DISR libraries from version 2022x to 2024x (upgrading profiles) is not available on Power'By.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd7e78b6-ea0a-4e98-bb73-e0ed5c2cbe1b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about working with the platform, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927113 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00604: L1 Node Types

- page_id: `191927113`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927113/L1+Node+Types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:00:38.489+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l1-node-types', 'l1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

62232222

62232224

62232223

**Description**

The L1 Node Types view specifies the types of logical Node used in the architecture. The nodes can be organized into a specialization hierarchy (subtype-supertype). An L1 view is used to define all the Nodes that will appear in a Logical Architecture. Nodes are elements of capability that are assembled and orchestrated in Logical Architecture. The levels of capability provided by each node are expressed as Measures of Performance, and these may be dependent on the environments in which the Node is required to operate.

The L1 view may be used to:

- Initial set up of a Logical Architecture.
- Define measure of performance for requirements specification purposes.
- Define the types of environment in which Nodes may operate.

**Implementation**

To create an L1 view, you can use one of the following diagrams:

- L1 Node Types diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

- L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

- L1 Free FormNode Types diagram.

62232221

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Performer' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Port' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Interface' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exhibits' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e661193d-70b3-4d18-852b-38fb3644ae65"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">62232222</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="556436e7-20f4-4d03-aad0-2413e1ea56cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">62232224</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a114aef9-c742-4287-bdf7-3015bd7de463"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">62232223</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The L1 Node Types view specifies the types of logical Node used in the architecture. The nodes can be organized into a specialization hierarchy (subtype-supertype). An L1 view is used to define all the Nodes that will appear in a Logical Architecture. Nodes are elements of capability that are assembled and orchestrated in Logical Architecture. The levels of capability provided by each node are expressed as Measures of Performance, and these may be dependent on the environments in which the Node is required to operate.</p><p>The L1 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Initial set up of a Logical Architecture.</li><li>Define measure of performance for requirements specification purposes.</li><li>Define the types of environment in which Nodes may operate.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>To create an L1 view, you can use one of the following diagrams:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link">L1 Node Types diagram.</span><span class="confluence-link"><br /><ac:image ac:title="L1 Node Types" ac:alt="L1 Node Types"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L1_node_types.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="L1 Node Types" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ul><ul><li><span class="confluence-link">L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram.</span><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><br /><ac:image ac:title="L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description" ac:alt="L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L1_high_level.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="L1 Node Types" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></span></span></li></ul><ul><li><span class="confluence-link" style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4fb7438c-2304-4b52-8884-f022763de8be">L1 Free Form </ac:inline-comment-marker><span class="confluence-link"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4fb7438c-2304-4b52-8884-f022763de8be">Node Types diagram</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><ac:inline-comment-marker <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927139 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00605: L2 Logical Internal Scenario

- page_id: `191927139`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927139/L2+Logical+Internal+Scenario
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:46:06.597+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l2-logical-scenario', 'l2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

63480270

63480272

63480271

**Description**

The L2i Logical Internal Scenario view shows, at a high level, the interactions between logical nodes and places them in the context of location(s). The primary purpose of the L2i view is to specify nodes (elements of capability) in context of each other. The context is usually expressed in terms of the information that flows between the nodes (e.g. the information flow requirements between capabilities in a given scenario). The context may also be flows of material, human resource or energy.

The L2i view may be used to:

- Define operational concepts.
- Elaborate capability requirements.
- Define collaboration needs.
- Associate capability with a location.
- Define problem space.
- Operational planning.
- Supply chain analysis.

**Implementation**

The L2i view can be represented using an L2i diagram which is based on theUML Composite Structure diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

63480269

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Role' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Known Resource' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Connector' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Port' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Measurement' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

## PAGE 00606: L2 Logical Scenario

- page_id: `191927121`

- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-29T08:44:37.690+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The L2 Logical Scenario view identifies the operational exchange requirements between nodes. It defines operational architecture and exchange requirements necessary to support a specific set of Capabilities.

**Implementation**

The L2 view canberepresented using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L2 Logical Scenario diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the L2 Logical Scenario diagram.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927184 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00607: L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy

- page_id: `191927184`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927184/L2-L3+Logical+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:48:17.375+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1977687974

1977687976

1977687975

**Description**

The L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy shows the main Operational Performers of the architectural scenario. It also illustrates the flows of information and materiel between these Operational Performers specified in the Operational Information model.

**Implementation**

The L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] . The primary purpose of the L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram is to define capability requirements within an operational context. It may also be used to express a capability boundary. The L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram can be used to show flows of funding, personnel and materiel in addition to information. The intended usage of the L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram includes:
  - Definition of operational concepts.
  - Elaboration of capability requirements.
  - Definition of collaboration needs.
  - Applying a local context to a capability.
  - Problem space definition.
  - Operational planning.
  - Supply chain analysis.
  - Allocation of activities to resources.

1977687973

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927143 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00608: L3 Node Interactions

- page_id: `191927143`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927143/L3+Node+Interactions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:01:17.280+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l3-node-interactions', 'l3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

64204026

64204029

64204027

**Description**

The L3 Node Interactions view addresses operational information exchanges between nodes. Information exchanges provide further detail of the interoperability requirements associated with the operational capability of interest. The focus is on information exchanges that cross the capability boundary.

Although the primary purpose of this view is to specify information exchanges, an L3 may also list flows of materiel, energy and human resources.

The L3 view may be used to:

- Define operational concepts.
- Elaborate capability requirements.
- Define collaboration needs.
- Associate capability with a location.
- Define Problem space.
- Operational planning.
- Analyze Supply chain.
- Secure models – e.g. domain-based security & entity trust models.

**Implementation**

The L3 view can be represented using:

- L3 Node Interactions table. [ATTACHMENT filename='L3.PNG']

- L3 Node Role Interactions table. [ATTACHMENT filename='L3_node_role_interactions.png']

- L3 spreadsheet report.

64204025

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Role' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Natural Resource' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927147 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00609: L4 Logical Activities

- page_id: `191927147`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927147/L4+Logical+Activities
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:55:43.659+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l4-logical-activities', 'l4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

64503150

64503152

64503151

**Description**

The L4 Logical Activities view describes the operations that are normally conducted in the course of achieving a mission or a business goal. It describes operational activities (or tasks), Input/Output flows between activities and to/from activities that are outside the scope of the Architecture. An operational activity is a logical process, specified independently of how it is carried out. To maintain this independence from implementation, logical nodes in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Internal Scenario' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] are used to represent the structure which carries out the operational activities. Operational activities are realized as functions (P4) which are the “how” to the Operational Activities’ “what”. That is, they are specified in terms of the resources that carry them out.

The L4 activity models describe the business processes associated with the architecture, as well as the:

- Relationships or dependencies among the business processes.
- Information exchanged between business processes.
- External interchanges (from/to business processes that are outside the scope of the model).

The L4 view may be used to: 
 • Clearly delineate lines of responsibility for activities when coupled with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Internal Scenario' space='UAF12P2024xR2']. 
 • Uncover unnecessary Operational Activity redundancy. 
 • Make decisions about streamlining, combining, or omitting activities. 
 • Define or flag issues, opportunities, or operational activities and their interactions (information flows among the activities) that need to be scrutinised further. 
 • Provide a necessary foundation for depicting activity sequencing and timing in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L8 Logical Constraints' space='UAF12P2024xR2'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L5 Logical States' space='UAF12P2024xR2'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L6 Logical Sequence' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

**Implementation**

The L4 view can be represented using:

- L4 Logical Activities diagram for Operational Activity Flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='L4_flow_model.png']

- L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='L4.png']
- L4 Logical Activities (BPD) diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Activity diagram.
- SysML Block diagram.
- SysML Activity diagram.

64503149

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Natural Resource' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927153 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00610: L5 Logical States

- page_id: `191927153`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927153/L5+Logical+States
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:56.313+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l5-logical-states', 'l5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

64826024

64826026

64826025

**Description**

The L5 Logical States view describes how operational nodes or activities respond to various events by changing state. The view represents the sets of events to which the architecture will respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

The L5 view can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. The L5 is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space='UAF12P2024xR2'].

**Implementation**

The L5 view can be represented using an UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

64826023

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational State Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MD2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927155 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00611: L6 Logical Sequence

- page_id: `191927155`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927155/L6+Logical+Sequence
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:56.387+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l6-logical-sequence', 'l6']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

65150934

65150936

65150935

**Description**

The L6 Logical Sequence view provides a time-ordered examination of the exchanges between participating Operational Nodes as a result of a particular scenario. Each event-trace diagram will have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

Operational Event-Trace Descriptions, sometimes called sequence diagrams, event scenarios or timing diagrams, allow the tracing of interactions between nodes in a scenario or critical sequence of events. The node interactions may correspond to flows of information, energy, material or people specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Internal Scenario' space='UAF12P2024xR2']. The L6 can be used by itself or in conjunction with an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L5 Logical States' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] to describe the dynamic behavior of nodes. The diagram below shows the components of an L6. The items across the top of the diagram are operational nodes.

Each node has a vertical time-line associated with it. Specific points in time can be labeled running down the left-hand side of the diagram. Directed lines between the node time lines represent interactions (e.g. information exchanges) between nodes, and the points at which they intersect the timeliness represent the times at which the nodes become aware of the events.

The L6 view may be used to:

- Analyze operational events.
- Sequences of interactions between nodes.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identify non-functional user requirements (input to URD).
- Operational test scenarios.

**Implementation**

The L6 view can be represented using a L6 diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

65150933

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Message' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Role' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

The node interactions may correspond to flows of information, energy, material or people specified in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L2 Logical Internal Scenario" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L2 - Logical Scenario]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The L6 can be used by itself or in conjunction with an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="L5 Logical States" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L5 - Logical States]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to describe the dynamic behavior of nodes. The diagram below shows the components of an L6. The items across the top of the diagram are operational nodes.</p><p>Each node has a vertical time-line associated with it. Specific points in time can be labeled running down the left-hand side of the diagram. Directed lines between the node time lines represent interactions (e.g. information exchanges) between nodes, and the points at which they intersect the timeliness represent the times at which the nodes become aware of the events.</p><p>The L6 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Analyze operational events.</li><li>Sequences of interactions between nodes.</li><li>Behavioral analysis.</li><li>Identify non-functional user requirements (input to URD).</li><li>Operational test scenarios.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The L6 view can be represented using a L6 diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="L6 Logical Sequence diagram" ac:alt="L6 Logical Sequence diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L6.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="L6 Logical Sequence" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="672498b2-ab6a-4239-bf68-325335c4e285"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">65150933</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Interaction" /></ac:link></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Message" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Role" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="fdc67782-e5fd-4e6a-b8b0-79d46e68f46b" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191927158 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00612: L7 Logical Data Model

- page_id: `191927158`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191927158/L7+Logical+Data+Model
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-07T07:41:56.412+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l7-logical-data-model', 'l7']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

65804908

65804910

65804909

**Description**

The L7 Logical Data Model view addresses the information perspective on an operational architecture. The L7 view is used to document the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information exchanges of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.

The L7 view may be used for:

- Information architecture.
- Information product hierarchy.

**Implementation**

The L7 view can be represented using:

- L7 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='L7.png']
- UML Class diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.

65804907

**Related elements**
