<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928450 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00642: Measurements

- page_id: `191928450`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928450/Measurements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:19:53.885+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A measurement is a property of an element representing something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure. You can create measurements either in the measurements table or on the element shape in the diagram .

Note, that when you display the measurements in the measurements compartment, you can choose to show the inherited names through the dot notation when the Property Set is applied as a type. In this case, the original name of the measurements will no longer be shown. However, if you need to see the original name, set the **Show Original Name of Measurement** symbol property to true. The original name will be displayed in brackets and in gray font (see the image bellow).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Learn more about working with measurements:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A measurement is a property of an element representing something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure. You can create measurements either in the measurements table or on the element shape in the diagram .</p><p>Note, that when you display the measurements in the measurements compartment, you can choose to show the inherited names through the dot notation when the Property Set is applied as a type. In this case, the original name of the measurements will no longer be shown. However, if you need to see the original name, set the <strong>Show Original Name of Measurement</strong> symbol property to true. The original name will be displayed in brackets and in gray font (see the image bellow).</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dot_notation_measurements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Measurements" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="original_measurement_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Measurements" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>Learn more about working with measurements:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="2c2b0623-cd0e-4255-b0a4-ebeb8a94756b" /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191928154 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00643: Measurements dialog

- page_id: `191928154`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191928154/Measurements+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-22T09:23:06.183+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['measurements-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1004957137

1004957139

1004957138

The **Measurements** dialog is designed to display measurements available to be added as columns to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] tables.

To open the **Measurements** dialog

- On the table toolbar, click Show Columns button and select Measurements . The Measurements dialog opens.

The **Measurements** dialog consists of two tabs:

- Operational Exchange (or **Resource****Exchange** in the SV-6 table).
- Operational Exchange Item (or Resource ExchangeItem in the SV-6 table).

The**Operational Exchange** (or **Resource****Exchange**in the SV-6 and table) tab displays measurements grouped by the Measurement Sets of the Operational Exchanges (or Resource Exchanges in the SV-6 and SvcV-6 tables). The **Operational Exchange Item** (or**Resource ExchangeItem** in the SV-6 table) tab displays measurements grouped by the Measurement Sets of Operational Exchange Items (or Resource Exchange Items in the SV-6 table). The measurements displayed in the **Measurements** dialog are collected from the active table.

The purpose of the Measurement Set is grouping the particular measurements. In order to show or hide measurements of a particular Measurement Set, you can expand or collapse the Measurement Set category. For example, Exchange Properties is the grouping Measurement Set of the measurements Accountability, Interoperability Level Achievable, and so forth.

When you select the measurement and click the **OK** button, the selected measurement is added as a new column into the table.

The selected measurement description is displayed in the Description area. If you want to see the description of your custom measurement, you should have specified the documentation when creating a measurement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1004957135

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying Custom Measurements' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929258 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00645: Measurements migration

- page_id: `191929258`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929258/Measurements+migration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-01-23T14:13:50.304+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If the UAF element is a Property Set (e.g. Operational Performer, Resource Performer, Information Element, Service Specification, Capability), the new measurement modeling approach is applied after the migration from UPDM 2.

The new approach includes:

- If an element has Property Set property with value (Measure Type or Measurement Set), then the new Measurement attribute is created under the element with the type Measure Type / Measurement Set.
- If an element has an Actual Property Set with value Actual Measurement Set , then the instance of that element is created. The instance gets a slot which defining feature is a Measurement attribute from the element. The value of that slot is the element which was set in the Actual Property Set property.
- Measurement attribute is created for each value in the Property Set property.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example for the measurements migration from UPDM 2 to UAF.

If an element is a measurable element (e.g. Operational Activity, Operational Exchange, Resource Role), the approach of applying measurements stays the same as in UPDM 2. Property Set property is renamed to Measurement Set. Actual Property Set property is renamed to Actual Property Set.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If the UAF element is a Property Set (e.g. Operational Performer, Resource Performer, Information Element, Service Specification, Capability), the new measurement modeling approach is applied after the migration from UPDM 2.</p><p>The new approach includes:</p><ul><li>If an element has Property Set property with value (Measure Type or Measurement Set), then the new Measurement attribute is created under the element with the type Measure Type / Measurement Set.</li><li>If an element has an Actual Property Set with value Actual Measurement Set , then the instance of that element is created. The instance gets a slot which defining feature is a Measurement attribute from the element. The value of that slot is the element which was set in the Actual Property Set property.</li><li>Measurement attribute is created for each value in the Property Set property.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_migration.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Measurements migration" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example for the measurements migration from UPDM 2 to UAF.</h6><p>If an element is a measurable element (e.g. Operational Activity, Operational Exchange, Resource Role), the approach of applying measurements stays the same as in UPDM 2. Property Set property is renamed to Measurement Set. Actual Property Set property is renamed to Actual Property Set.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929238 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00646: Measurements migration in UAF 1.2

- page_id: `191929238`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929238/Measurements+migration+in+UAF+1.2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2022-05-19T13:19:37.760+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.2 > UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2. Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If the UAF element is a Property Set (e.g. Operational Performer, Resource Performer, Information Element, Service Specification, Capability), the new measurement modeling approach is applied after the migration from UPDM 2.

The new approach includes:

- If an element has Property Set property with value (Measure Type or Measurement Set), then the new Measurement attribute is created under the element with the type Measure Type / Measurement Set.
- If an element has an Actual Property Set with value Actual Measurement Set , then the instance of that element is created. The instance gets a slot which defining feature is a Measurement attribute from the element. The value of that slot is the element which was set in the Actual Property Set property.
- Measurement attribute is created for each value in the Property Set property.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example for the measurements migration from UPDM 2 to UAF 1.2.

If an element is a measurable element (e.g. Operational Activity, Operational Exchange, Resource Role), the approach of applying measurements stays the same as in UPDM 2. Property Set property is renamed to Measurement Set. Actual Property Set property is renamed to Actual Property Set.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If the UAF element is a Property Set (e.g. Operational Performer, Resource Performer, Information Element, Service Specification, Capability), the new measurement modeling approach is applied after the migration from UPDM 2.</p><p>The new approach includes:</p><ul><li>If an element has Property Set property with value (Measure Type or Measurement Set), then the new Measurement attribute is created under the element with the type Measure Type / Measurement Set.</li><li>If an element has an Actual Property Set with value Actual Measurement Set , then the instance of that element is created. The instance gets a slot which defining feature is a Measurement attribute from the element. The value of that slot is the element which was set in the Actual Property Set property.</li><li>Measurement attribute is created for each value in the Property Set property.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_migration.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Measurements migration in UAF 1.2" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example for the measurements migration from UPDM 2 to UAF 1.2.</h6><p>If an element is a measurable element (e.g. Operational Activity, Operational Exchange, Resource Role), the approach of applying measurements stays the same as in UPDM 2. Property Set property is renamed to Measurement Set. Actual Property Set property is renamed to Actual Property Set.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929015 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00647: Metadata

- page_id: `191929015`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929015/Metadata
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-10-10T10:31:55.439+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A comment that can be applied to any element in the architecture. The attributes associated with this element details the relationship between the element and its related dublinCoreElement, metaDataScheme, category and name. This allows the element to be referenced using the Semantic Web.

**Architecture** **Framework**

UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0

**Extension**

UML Comment

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">A comment that can be applied to any element in the architecture. The attributes associated with this element details the relationship between the element and its related dublinCoreElement, metaDataScheme, category and name. This allows the element to be referenced using the Semantic Web.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">UAF 1.2, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Comment</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929185 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00648: Migrating Server Projects

- page_id: `191929185`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929185/Migrating+Server+Projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-06-19T13:42:03.945+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > Project migration issues on Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you are working with server projects, part of the elements might be locked by other users. You cannot migrate locked elements. The following procedure explains how to migrate a server project from DoDAF to UPDM 1.x.

To migrate a server project

1. In the Model Browser, open the **Locked Elements** tab to check if there are no elements locked by other users. If there are elements locked by other users, each user must unlock elements locked by him/her.Migration will be aborted if at least one element cannot be locked.
2. Perform the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DoDAF Projects Migration' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
3. Commit the project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>When you are working with server projects, part of the elements might be locked by other users. You cannot migrate locked elements. The following procedure explains how to migrate a server project from DoDAF to UPDM 1.x.</p><p><br /></p><p>To migrate a server project</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Model Browser, open the <strong>Locked Elements</strong> tab to check if there are no elements locked by other users.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3db086d4-c5c7-4f6c-8fcb-68f3472a4ca6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If there are elements locked by other users, each user must unlock elements locked by him/her.</p><p>Migration will be aborted if at least one element cannot be locked.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Perform the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="DoDAF Projects Migration" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[project migration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Commit the project.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

The duration of the projects migration from UAF 1.1 to UAF 1.2 depends on the project sizes. In the following chart, you can see the duration of local and Teamwork Cloud projects migration:

For the recommendations of RAM for the projects migration, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installation, licensing, and system requirements' space='NMDOC'].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929220 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00653: Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1

- page_id: `191929220`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929220/Migration+from+UPDM+3+UAF+1.0+beta+to+UAF+1.1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-04-07T12:36:21.137+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There is no direct migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1, so to migrate your projects, you need to do the following:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

##### Migrating to UAF 1.0

To migrate the UPDM 3 project to UAF 1.0

1. Open your UPDM 3 project with a modeling tool with UAF Plugin which supports UAF 1.0 profile version. For this step, you need to use the modeling tool and UAF Plugin of 19.0 version.
2. Save the project.

For more information about changes, please see chapter [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.0' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

##### Migrating to UAF 1.1

After you project is migrated to UAF 1.0, you can migrate it to UAF 1.1. For more information, please see chapter [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.1' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There is no direct migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1, so to migrate your projects, you need to do the following:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Migrating to UAF 1.0"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[First, you need to migrate your project to UAF 1.0. ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Migrating to UAF 1.1"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Then you can migrate that project to UAF 1.1.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:content-title="Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul><h3>Migrating to UAF 1.0</h3><p>To migrate the UPDM 3 project to UAF 1.0</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Open your UPDM 3 project with a modeling tool with UAF Plugin which supports UAF 1.0 profile version. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6acb6dfe-1750-475f-bab8-7fe3ff6c30d2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For this step, you need to use the modeling tool and UAF Plugin of 19.0 version.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Save the project.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>For more information about changes, please see chapter <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.0" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p><h3>Migrating to UAF 1.1</h3><p>After you project is migrated to UAF 1.0, you can migrate it to UAF 1.1. For more information, please see chapter <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.1" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></p>
````

Dependency

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191929260 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00656: Missing data in diagrams, matrices, tables, and relation maps

- page_id: `191929260`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191929260/Missing+data+in+diagrams+matrices+tables+and+relation+maps
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-05-12T10:00:17.941+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Since UPDM 2 profile [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removed elements' space='UAF12P2024xR2'], the Element Type of matrices, tables, and relation maps becomes empty after the migration. The same goes for the customized elements derived from the removed UPDM2 elements. If the UPDM2 [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Replaced and renamed elements' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] to the UAF element, the UAF element is set as an Element Type (criteria).

#### NOTE: Exception

Exception

If Operational Event Trace, Resource Event Trace, Service Interaction was set as an Element Type criteria, after the migration an Interaction replaces those elements.

After the migration from UPDM 2 to UAF, some tags are no longer supported. During the migration process, all the information from those tags is moved to the**To Do**property of the tag owner. To find this property, open the appropriate element's Specification window. You can display the tag information in the note, anchored to the element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Removed tag added to ToDo property

Condition::conditionKind tag was removed, so after the migration it will be added into ToDo property in the Specification window of the Condition element.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>Since UPDM 2 profile <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Removed elements" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[elements were removed]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, the Element Type of matrices, tables, and relation maps becomes empty after the migration. The same goes for the customized elements derived from the removed UPDM2 elements. If the UPDM2 <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Replaced and renamed elements" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[element is migrated]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the UAF element, the UAF element is set as an Element Type (criteria).</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f830871-f7d3-43f9-a110-feeded1958ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Exception</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If Operational Event Trace, <span>Resource Event Trace, <span>Service Interaction was set as an Element Type criteria, after the migration an Interaction replaces those elements.</span></span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">After the migration from UPDM 2 to UAF, some tags are no longer supported. During the migration process, all the information from those tags is moved to the </span><strong>To Do</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> property of the tag owner. To find this property, open the appropriate element's Specification window. You can display the tag information in the note, anchored to the element.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="migration_tagged_values.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Missing data in diagrams, matrices, tables, and relation maps" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h3>Removed tag added to ToDo property</h3><p>Condition::conditionKind tag was removed, so after the migration it will be added into ToDo property in the Specification window of the Condition element.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p>
````

During the migration process, all the information from those tags is moved to the </span><strong>To Do</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> property of the tag owner. To find this property, open the appropriate element's Specification window. You can display the tag information in the note, anchored to the element.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="migration_tagged_values.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Missing data in diagrams, matrices, tables, and relation maps in UAF 1.2" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h3>Removed tag added to ToDo property</h3><p>Condition::conditionKind tag was removed, so after the migration it will be added into ToDo property in the Specification window of the Condition element.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926735 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00659: MODAF

- page_id: `191926735`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926735/MODAF
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:57:05.126+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

According to modeling needs, there are the following MODAF templates for different purposes:

- **MODAF Project**- creating a MODAF project will switch the application to the MODAF graphical user interface and use the MODAF model templates.
- **MODAF Project Template with Process Guide**- using this template, you will get a new MODAF project enhanced with a modeling guide. The modeling guide helps you to build the architecture by providing summary information about the MODAF structure and the architecture creation process.

All MODAF views are described in the following sections:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">According to modeling needs, there are the following MODAF templates for different purposes:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>MODAF Project</strong><span> </span>- creating a MODAF project will switch the application to the MODAF graphical user interface and use the MODAF model templates.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>MODAF Project Template with Process Guide</strong><span> </span>- using this template, you will get a new MODAF project enhanced with a modeling guide. The modeling guide helps you to build the architecture by providing summary information about the MODAF structure and the architecture creation process.</span></li></ul><p>All MODAF views are described in the following sections:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="a00715aa-d17f-4574-86dd-b775749c82d7" /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926736 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00661: MODAF All Views Viewpoint

- page_id: `191926736`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926736/MODAF+All+Views+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:01:02.409+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

411478012

411478014

411478013

Elements that are part of the All View. The All-Views (AVs) provide an overarching description of the architecture, its scope, ownership, timeframe and all of the other meta data that is required in order to effectively search and query architectural models. They also provide a place to record any findings arising from the architecting process. The AVs include a dictionary of the terms used in the construction of the architecture – which helps others fully understand its meaning at a later date. Since the AVs provide critical information for the future access and exploitation of an architectural model their population is essential whenever an architecture is created or modified. The AVs provide a critical input into the processes that provide architectural governance.

411478011

**Views**

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eae6900d-4abc-48ff-8928-fe4a521883aa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">411478012</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d9e68a50-db09-4b98-a5e4-df119c295cc7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">411478014</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6a2a0e2-44a2-453a-b175-4f68e432e098"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">411478013</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Elements that are part of the All View. The All-Views (AVs) provide an overarching description of the architecture, its scope, ownership, timeframe and all of the other meta data that is required in order to effectively search and query architectural models. They also provide a place to record any findings arising from the architecting process. The AVs include a dictionary of the terms used in the construction of the architecture – which helps others fully understand its meaning at a later date. Since the AVs provide critical information for the future access and exploitation of an architectural model their population is essential whenever an architecture is created or modified. The AVs provide a critical input into the processes that provide architectural governance.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ddb43cbf-1af3-4151-8538-99e7bf33980b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">411478011</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="a3f29e67-0ea5-4b50-b4d6-ff0d3a514e84" /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926779 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00662: MODAF Operational Viewpoint

- page_id: `191926779`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926779/MODAF+Operational+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T13:44:33.430+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

413846051

413846053

413846052

The Operational Viewpoint is about real-world activities, the people and machinery that perform them, and the means by which they are performed. The Operational Viewpoint is divided into nine views intended to answer the “who”, “what”, “when”, “where”, “why”, and “how” of a mission.

The Operational Views are common to MODAF and DoDAF; OV-1b, OV-1c, and OV-2 however, have been customized to provide for MOD requirements. The Operational Views describe the tasks and activities, operational elements, and information exchanges required to conduct operations. In MODAF thinking, the OV Views are considered to illustrate the Logical Architecture of the enterprise.

413846050

**View**

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The Operational Viewpoint is divided into nine views intended to answer the “who”, “what”, “when”, “where”, “why”, and “how” of a mission.</p><p>The Operational Views are common to MODAF and DoDAF; OV-1b, OV-1c, and OV-2 however, have been customized to provide for MOD requirements. The Operational Views describe the tasks and activities, operational elements, and information exchanges required to conduct operations. In MODAF thinking, the OV Views are considered to illustrate the Logical Architecture of the enterprise.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51021151-7b24-4f28-ab31-f3d730d0b90f"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jrpPuZ8Vf74" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8cbd928-d9b8-44d5-a428-f1c17ff35954"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">413846050</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>View</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7c989114-0d1c-4195-a04c-eabf736fc9cc" /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

417346697

The Services View (SOV) shows the elements that are part of SOV-1 through SOV-5.

The Service-Orientated View is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the Operational View. A service is described as a unit of work through which a particular Resource provides a useful result to a consuming Resource.

411757999

411758001

411758000

The Strategic View (StV) shows the elements that are part of StV-1 through StV-6. The Strategic Elements are used in the Strategic View that provides an overall Enterprise Architecture assessment of the Capabilities and their relationships facilitating Capability Management (e.g. capability introduction, integration, re-alignment and removal). While an Enterprise will have a number of UAF Architecture Descriptions that have the Operational, System, Technical Standards, and All Views, only one Strategic View will exist across a number of Architecture Descriptions.

411757997

**Views**

## PAGE 00665: MODAF Systems Viewpoint

- page_id: `191926838`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926838/MODAF+Systems+Viewpoint

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

419208934

419208936

419208935

The System View (SV) shows the elements that are part of SV-1 through SV-12. Models in the System Viewpoint represent alternate realizations in terms of equipment capability of the operational capabilities expressed through models in the Operational Viewpoint and in the User Requirements. The System Viewpoint primarily addresses the specification of the system capability needed (rather than implementation details). Significant changes originally made in MODAF improved the ability for modelers to represent configuration of capability that include people as well as systems and platforms.

419208933

**Views**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926889 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00666: MODAF Technical Standards Viewpoint

- page_id: `191926889`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926889/MODAF+Technical+Standards+Viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-30T14:04:45.663+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

418729522

418729524

418729523

The Technical View (TV) shows the elements that are part of TV-1 through TV-2.

The Technical View is a set of views delineating standards, rules, notations, and conventions that apply to the implementation of the system architecture. When the standards profile is tied to the system elements to which they apply, TV-1 serves as the bridge between the SV and TV. SV-9 forecasts relate to the TV-1 in that a timed technology forecast may contribute to the decision to retire or phase out the use of a certain standard in connection with a system element. Similarly, SV-9 forecasts relate to TV-2 standards forecasts in that a certain standard may be adopted depending on a certain technology becoming available (e.g., the availability of Java Script may influence the decision to adopt a new HTML standard).

MODAF extends the core DoDAF Technical Standards Views to include non-technical standards and policies applicable to the architecture such as operational doctrine, industry process standards, etc. Additionally, the TV-1 may also document policies and standards applicable to the operational or business context. MODAF also distinguishes between ‘applicability’ and ‘conformance’ with regard to architectural elements. If a standard is applicable to a given architecture, that architecture need not be fully conformant with the standard. The degree of conformance to a given standard may be judged on a risk basis at an approval point. An association between a Standard and an architectural element is not to be interpreted as stating the level of compliance of the element is fully compliant with that Standard. Additional evidences would need to be given (outside MODAF) to confirm the level of compliance. Finally, MODAF adds the explicit requirement that any Standards cited in TV-1 View must, where appropriate, be in accordance with the trend towards open architectures – i.e. standards which encourage stove-piped systems are expressly prohibited.

418729521

**Views**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24d14305-3e75-4bd1-b841-4581d318f48f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418729522</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb0ba76b-4983-43c4-85b7-1a23b08e652d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418729524</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab7dcaaf-de79-467a-a5ae-4ac55bbd6721"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418729523</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Technical View (TV) shows the elements that are part of TV-1 through TV-2.</p><p>The Technical View is a set of views delineating standards, rules, notations, and conventions that apply to the implementation of the system architecture. When the standards profile is tied to the system elements to which they apply, TV-1 serves as the bridge between the SV and TV. SV-9 forecasts relate to the TV-1 in that a timed technology forecast may contribute to the decision to retire or phase out the use of a certain standard in connection with a system element. Similarly, SV-9 forecasts relate to TV-2 standards forecasts in that a certain standard may be adopted depending on a certain technology becoming available (e.g., the availability of Java Script may influence the decision to adopt a new HTML standard).</p><p>MODAF extends the core DoDAF Technical Standards Views to include non-technical standards and policies applicable to the architecture such as operational doctrine, industry process standards, etc. Additionally, the TV-1 may also document policies and standards applicable to the operational or business context. MODAF also distinguishes between ‘applicability’ and ‘conformance’ with regard to architectural elements. If a standard is applicable to a given architecture, that architecture need not be fully conformant with the standard. The degree of conformance to a given standard may be judged on a risk basis at an approval point. An association between a Standard and an architectural element is not to be interpreted as stating the level of compliance of the element is fully compliant with that Standard. Additional evidences would need to be given (outside MODAF) to confirm the level of compliance. Finally, MODAF adds the explicit requirement that any Standards cited in TV-1 View must, where appropriate, be in accordance with the trend towards open architectures – i.e. standards which encourage stove-piped systems are expressly prohibited.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eab04b60-7f6b-4780-b0ad-0b6684437e20"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418729521</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="256e754f-3204-47a1-bb84-748b710aaf8c" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926771 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00667: MODAF. AcV-1 Acquisition Clusters

- page_id: `191926771`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926771/MODAF.+AcV-1+Acquisition+Clusters
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-21T11:38:49.025+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Acquisition Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

413387346

413387349

413387348

**Description**

The Acquisition Clusters (AcV-1) View describes how acquisition projects are grouped in organisational terms as a coherent portfolio of acquisition programmes.

The AcV-1 View provides a way of describing the organizational relationships between multiple acquisition projects, each of which is responsible for delivering individual systems or capabilities. By definition, this View covers acquisition programmes consisting of multiple projects and will generally not be developed by those building Architectures for an individual project. In essence, the AcV-1 is an organizational breakdown consisting of actual organizations (see OV-4). The view is strongly linked with the StV-4 which shows capability clusters and dependencies.

The AcV-1 View is hierarchical in nature. Higher level groupings of projects (or, rather the organisations that own these projects) form acquisition clusters.

The intent of an AcV-1 View Product is to show:

- All of the acquisition projects delivering systems or system of systems (SoS) within the acquisition programmes under consideration.
- Other systems and SoS which may have a bearing on the Architecture.
- How the systems will be best integrated into acquisition clusters.
- The nesting of acquisition clusters to form a hierarchy.

**Implementation**

AcV-1 can be represented using:

- AcV-1 Acquisition Clusters diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- AcV-1 Responsibility Matrix which is an editable Dependency Matrix.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### AcV-1 Responsibility Matrix

**Related views**

In essence, the AcV-1 is an organizational breakdown consisting of actual organizations (see OV-4). The view is strongly linked with the StV-4 which shows capability clusters and dependencies.

413387345

## PAGE 00668: MODAF. AcV-2 Programme Timelines

- page_id: `191926775`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926775/MODAF.+AcV-2+Programme+Timelines
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T13:08:38.719+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Acquisition Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

413597688

413597690

413597689

**Description**

The AcV-2 view provides a timeline perspective on programmes.

The AcV-2 View is intended primarily to support the acquisition and fielding processes including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of all the DLODs to achieve a successfully integrated military capability.

For capability-based procurement, these work streams might conveniently be equated with Defence Lines of Development (DLODs).

**Implementation**

AcV-2 can be represented using an AcV-2 diagram which is realized as a Gantt Chart.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Acv-2 Programme Timelines

**Related views**

The use of AcV-2 should support the management of capability delivery and be aligned with the capability phasing view (StV-3), if one exists.

## PAGE 00669: MODAF. AV-1 Overview and Summary Information

- page_id: `191926737`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926737/MODAF.+AV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T12:33:37.103+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF All Views Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

411544091

411544094

411544092

**Description**

The overview and summary information contained within the AV-1 product provides an executive-level summary information in a consistent form that allows for quick reference and comparison between architectural descriptions. AV-1 includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture. In an enterprise repository environment, individual architectures are mapped against enterprise phases to provide context between the architectures. AV-1 is usually a structured text product. Organizations may create a template for the AV-1 that can then be used to create a consistent set of information across different architecture-based projects.

**Implementation**

AV-1 can be represented:

- Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial AV-1 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.
- Using a AV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of AV-1 Overview and Summary Information report

411544089

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Architectural Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Architecture Metadata' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing reports' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

In an enterprise repository environment, individual architectures are mapped against enterprise phases to provide context between the architectures. AV-1 is usually a structured text product. Organizations may create a template for the AV-1 that can then be used to create a consistent set of information across different architecture-based projects.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>AV-1 can be represented:</p><ul><li>Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. ## PAGE 00670: MODAF. AV-2 Integrated Dictionary

- page_id: `191926739`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926739/MODAF.+AV-2+Integrated+Dictionary
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-09-15T12:34:54.887+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF All Views Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

411641246

411641249

411641248

**Description**

An AV-2 presents all the Elements used in an architecture as a standalone structure. An AV-2 presents all the Elements as a specialization hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element. An AV-2 shows elements from the Ontology that have been used in the architecture and new elements that have been introduced by the architecture. Architectures often introduce new terms – usually because the architecture is covering new technology or business processes. The purpose of the AV-2 is to provide a local extension of the Ontology to explain the terms and abbreviations used in building the architecture.

**Implementation**

AV-2 can be represented:

- Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data.
- An AV-2 table. If you have AV-2 diagrams from previous versions, they will be migrated into the AV-1 diagram when loading the project with UPDM 17.0.2.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### AV-2 Integrated Dictionary

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of AV-2 Integrated Dictionary report

411641245

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Definition' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Same As' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926780 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00671: MODAF. OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic

- page_id: `191926780`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926780/MODAF.+OV-1+High-Level+Operational+Concept+Graphic
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-21T11:39:33.768+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

413963572

413963574

413963573

**Description**

The purpose of High-level Operational Concept Graphic is to provide a high-level graphical and textual description of operational concept (high level organizations, missions, geographic configuration, connectivity, etc) of what the architecture is supposed to do, and how it is supposed to do it. The OV-1, along with the corresponding AV-1 product is intended to serve as an executive summary of the architecture.

**Implementation**

OV-1 can be represented using:

- Link to an external document.
- OV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- OV-1 Free Form diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure Diagram.

**Sample** 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic

413963571

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='High Level Operational Concept' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Concept Role' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-1 diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e35b1610-0cee-4076-a5ed-d0def3f167aa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">413963572</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3e0ad22-5783-4a96-8734-896c2a9f7f21"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">413963574</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c99fc90b-5ad0-4aea-8249-c49816a87841"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">413963573</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The purpose of High-level Operational Concept Graphic is to provide a high-level graphical and textual description of operational concept (high level organizations, missions, geographic configuration, connectivity, etc) of what the architecture is supposed to do, and how it is supposed to do it. The OV-1, along with the corresponding AV-1 product is intended to serve as an executive summary of the architecture.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>OV-1 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>Link to an external document.</li><li>OV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.</li><li>OV-1 Free Form diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>UML Composite Structure Diagram.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OV_1.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="MODAF. OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83312f6d-985d-4bf9-8c57-d6afd0ab57ec"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">413963571</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="High Level Operational Concept" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Concept Role" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating OV-1 diagram" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926783 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00672: MODAF. OV-2 Operational Node Internal Relationship Description

- page_id: `191926783`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926783/MODAF.+OV-2+Operational+Node+Internal+Relationship+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-21T11:42:58.394+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

414117534

414117536

414117535

**Implementation**

OV-2 can be represented using:

- OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### OV-2 Operational Node Internal Relationship Description

**Related views**

An OV-2 is highly related with an OV-5. Operational Nodes shown in the OV-2 are the performers of the Operational Activities modeled in the OV-5. OV-2 focuses on the Operational Nodes, with the activities being a secondary adornment. The OV-5, on the other hand, places first-order attention on operational activities and only second-order attention on Nodes, which can be shown as annotations or swim-lanes on the activities.

Information flows can be modeled either in the OV-2 or OV-5. In both cases they are highly associated and in general should be reused between these views.

The OV-2 displays the Capabilities required by Nodes from StV-2. That is an association between two abstraction levels of user requirements where the OV is more specific than the StV and a Node is more specific concept than a Capability.

The other important mapping is between OV-2 and SV-1. The specification Node and implementation Resource are subjects to map here. One OV-2 product can have several implementations in the SV-1.

414117533

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Known Resource' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Connector' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Port' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Role' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Measurement' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926785 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00673: MODAF. OV-2 Operational Node Relationship Description

- page_id: `191926785`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926785/MODAF.+OV-2+Operational+Node+Relationship+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-08-30T13:20:52.641+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

414271992

414271994

414271993

**Description**

The Operational Node Relationship Description tracks the need to exchange information from specific operational nodes (that play a key role in the architecture) to others. An OV-2 does not depict the connectivity between the nodes. MODAF modifies the OV-2 in two ways. First, it recommends that an OV-2 diagram (now OV2a) shows the platforms or geographic locations at which operational nodes are deployed. Secondly, it provides additional information (OV-2b) about each needline in the form of a requirements specification. There are now four types of needlines identified as follows:

1. InformationExchange
2. EnergyFlow
3. MaterielFlow
4. MovementOfPeople

In addition, MODAF permits service-oriented architectures. Instead of needlines between nodes, it is possible to show which services the nodes provide and consume. Finally, MODAF again permits known resources to be shown in an OV-2. However, this must be clearly shown as a KnownResource in an OV-2 model. LogicalArchitecture, which is the container class for all the nodes and KnownResources, is introduced.

**Implementation**

OV-2 can be represented using:

- OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- OV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### OV-2 Operational Node Relationship Description

**Related views**

An OV-2 is highly related with an OV-5. Operational Nodes shown in the OV-2 are the performers of the Operational Activities modeled in the OV-5. OV-2 focuses on the Operational Nodes, with the activities being a secondary adornment. The OV-5, on the other hand, places first-order attention on operational activities and only second-order attention on Nodes, which can be shown as annotations or swimlanes on the activities.

Information flows can be modeled either in the OV-2 or OV-5. In both cases they are highly associated and in general should be reused between these views.

The OV-2 displays the Capabilities required by Nodes from StV-2. That is an association between two abstraction levels of user requirements where the OV is more specific than the StV and a Node is more specific concept than a Capability.

The other important mapping is between OV-2 and SV-1. The specification Node and implementation Resource are subjects to map here. One OV-2 product can have several implementations in the SV-1.

414271991

**Related elements**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926788 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00674: MODAF. OV-3 Operational Information Exchange Matrix

- page_id: `191926788`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926788/MODAF.+OV-3+Operational+Information+Exchange+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-03-12T08:25:15.732+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Information exchanges express the relationship across the three basic architecture data elements of an OV (Operational Activities, Operational Performers, and Information Flow) with a focus on the specific aspects of the Information Flow and the information content.

The Information Exchanges of the OV-3 should remain at a high level of aggregation to represent actual information workflow products that are used at the Operational Performers shown in the OV-2 (and not their subordinate Operational Performers).

**Implementation**

OV-3 can be represented using:

- OV-3 table.
- OV-3 role-based table. A role-based table represents exchanges between Operational Roles.
- OV-3 spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### OV-3 Operational Information Exchange Matrix

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### OV-3 Role-based Operational Information Exchange Matrix

**Related views**

An OV-3 is initially constructed from the information contained in the Operational Node Relationship Description (OV-2).

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Performer' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Natural Resource' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with OV-3 table' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>Information exchanges express the relationship across the three basic architecture data elements of an OV (Operational Activities, Operational Performers, and Information Flow) with a focus on the specific aspects of the Information Flow and the information content.</p><p>The Information Exchanges of the OV-3 should remain at a high level of aggregation to represent actual information workflow products that are used at the Operational Performers shown in the OV-2 (and not their subordinate Operational Performers).</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>OV-3 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>OV-3 table.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">OV-3 role-based table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95fc2252-c5b7-4d99-813f-69e414365fe0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A role-based table represents exchanges between Operational Roles.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>OV-3 spreadsheet report.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="MODAF.ov-3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="MODAF. OV-3 Operational Information Exchange Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>OV-3 Operational Information Exchange Matrix</h6><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="MODAF.OV-3_role-based.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="MODAF. OV-3 Operational Information Exchange Matrix" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>OV-3 Role-based Operational Information Exchange Matrix</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>An OV-3 is initially constructed from the information contained in the Operational Node Relationship Description (OV-2).</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Performer" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Information" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Natural Resource" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Artifact" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Post" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Software" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Capability Configuration" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Organization" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Activity" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with OV-3 table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926793 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00675: MODAF. OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart

- page_id: `191926793`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926793/MODAF.+OV-4+Organizational+Relationships+Chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-02-21T11:44:20.635+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

414660137

414660139

414660138

**Description**

The Organizational Relationships Chart illustrates the command structure or relationships (as opposed to relationships with respect to a business process flow) among human roles, organizations, or organization types that are the key players in architecture. MODAF divides The OV-4 in two views: an OV-4 Typical and an OV-4 Actual. The former is exactly as the DoDAF OV-4, while the latter is a special form of the SV-1; where the resources are restricted to being organizational.

**Implementation**

OV-4 can be represented using:

- OV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart

**Related views**

The organizations that are modeled using an OV-4 in the Operational Viewpoint may also appear in other views, for example, SV-1 (organizational constituents of a capability configuration), AcV-1 (actual organizations that own projects), and SV-5 (organizational resources performs functions).

414660136

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926796 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00676: MODAF. OV-5 Operational Activity Model

- page_id: `191926796`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926796/MODAF.+OV-5+Operational+Activity+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-07-24T14:13:36.008+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

414777526

414777528

414777527

**Description**

The Operational Activity Model describes the operations that are normally conducted in the course of achieving a mission or a business goal, from a net-centric perspective. It describes capabilities, operational activities (or tasks), input and output (I/O) flows between activities, and I/O flows to/from activities that are outside the scope of the architecture. It is imperative that the levels-of-detail between the OV-2, OV-3, and OV-5 remain cohesive. For example, if one diagram of OV-2 operational nodes is developed that shows aggregated organizations only, then it is imperative that the corresponding OV-5 product be developed to show only those operational activities that are meaningful with respect to these operational nodes. Similarly, the information exchanges of OV-3 should remain at a high level of aggregation to represent actual information workflow products that are used at the operational nodes depicted in OV-2 (and not their subordinate operational nodes). The net-centric OV-5 may be used in the following ways:

- Delineate lines of dependency on external activities when coupled with an OV-2.
- Highlight information flows to depict the status of the information's refinement (raw, preprocessed, fused, etc.).
- Provide the critical foundation for depicting Task, Post, Process, and Use (TPPU) activity sequencing and timing in the OV-6a, OV-6b, and OV-6c.
- Identify critical mission threads and operational information exchanges by annotating which activities are critical, i.e., identify the activities in the model that are critical.

**Implementation**

OV-5 can be represented using:

- OV-5 diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram.
- OV-5 diagram for Operational Activity flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Activity diagram.
- SysML Block diagram.
- SysML Activity diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### OV-5 Operational Activity hierarchy Model

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of OV-5 Operational Activity Model

**Related views**

The OV-5 and OV-2 are, to a degree, complements of each other. An OV-5 focuses on the operational activities whereas OV-2 focuses on the operational nodes. Due to the relationship between nodes and operational activities, these types of view should normally be developed together.

To maintain this independence from implementation, the logical Nodes in an OV-2 are used to represent the structure which carries out the Operational Activities. Operational Activities are realized as Functions (SV-4) which are the “how” to the Operational Activities’ “what”, for example, they are specified in terms of the resources that carry them out.

The activities described in an OV-5 may be Standard Operational Activities which are defined in the StV-6 (which also maps the activities to corresponding capabilities).

414777524

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Parameter' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard Operational Activity' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Performer' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Is Capable To Perform' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity Action' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity Edge' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Natural Resource' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5 Operational Activity Model diagram' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying possible Operational Exchanges on the selected Operational Activity Edge' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

Operational Activities are realized as Functions (SV-4) which are the “how” to the Operational Activities’ “what”, for example, they are specified in terms of the resources that carry them <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926802 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00677: MODAF. OV-6a Operational Rules Model

- page_id: `191926802`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926802/MODAF.+OV-6a+Operational+Rules+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-19T08:08:25.271+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

414963391

414963393

414963392

**Description**

The Operational Rules Model specifies operational or business rules that are constraints on an enterprise, a mission, operation, business, or an architecture. While other OV views (e.g., OV-1, OV-2, and OV-5) describe the structure of a business—what the business can do—for the most part, they do not describe what the business must do, or what it cannot do. At the mission level, an OV-6a may consist of doctrine, guidance, rules of engagement, and so forth. At the operation level, rules may include such things as a military Operational Plan (OPLAN). At lower levels, an OV-6a describes the rules under which the architecture or its nodes behave under specified conditions. Such rules can be expressed in a textual form, for example, “If (these conditions) exist, and (this event) occurs, then (perform these actions).” At a top level, rules should at least embody the concepts of operations defined in an OV-1, and should provide guidelines for the development and definition of more detailed 
rules and behavioral definitions that will occur later in the architecture definition process.

**Implementation**

OV-6a can be represented using:

- OV-6a table.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-6a Operational Node Parametric' space='UAF12P2024xR2'] .
- OV-6a spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### OV-6a Operational Rules Model

**Related views**

An OV-6a constrains the structure elements of OV-1, OV-2, and OV-5. OV-6a can also be used to extend the capture of business requirements by constraining the structure and validity of the OV-7 elements.

As the View name implies, the rules captured in an OV-6a are operational (i.e., mission-oriented) whereas resource-oriented rules are defined in an SV-10 (OV-6 is the “what” to SV-10’s “how”).

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the**Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

414963390

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Constraint' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Performer' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with OV-6a table' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b8f011d-12dd-434c-878b-a8195e987319"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">414963391</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be88cf91-7917-46e1-b246-862829184592"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">414963393</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3aa10230-e676-4ad6-8946-c93ecd2e2435"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">414963392</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Operational Rules Model specifies operational or business rules that are constraints on an enterprise, a mission, operation, business, or an architecture. While other OV views (e.g., OV-1, OV-2, and OV-5) describe the structure of a business—what the business can do—for the most part, they do not describe what the business must do, or what it cannot do. At the mission level, an OV-6a may consist of doctrine, guidance, rules of engagement, and so forth. At the operation level, rules may include such things as a military Operational Plan (OPLAN). At lower levels, an OV-6a describes the rules under which the architecture or its nodes behave under specified conditions. Such rules can be expressed in a textual form, for example, “If (these conditions) exist, and (this event) occurs, then (perform these actions).” At a top level, rules should at least embody the concepts of operations defined in an OV-1, and should provide guidelines for the development and definition of more detailed<br />rules and behavioral definitions that will occur later in the architecture definition process.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>OV-6a can be represented using:</p><ul><li>OV-6a table.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="OV-6a Operational Node Parametric" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-6a Operational Node Parametric diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>OV-6a spreadsheet report.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ov_6a.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="MODAF. OV-6a Operational Rules Model" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>OV-6a Operational Rules Model</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>An OV-6a constrains the structure elements of OV-1, OV-2, and OV-5. OV-6a can also be used to extend the capture of business requirements by constraining the structure and validity of the OV-7 elements.</p><p>As the View name implies, the rules captured in an OV-6a are operational (i.e., mission-oriented) whereas resource-oriented rules are defined in an SV-10 (OV-6 is the “what” to SV-10’s “how”).</p><p style=""><strong>Constraint owner</strong></p><p style="">You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the<span> </span><strong>Project Options</strong>.</p><p style="">To select a constraint owner</p><hr style="" /><ol style=""><li>In the main menu, click<span> </span><strong>Options</strong><span> </span>&gt;<span> </span><strong>Project</strong>. The<span> </span><strong>Project Options</strong><span> </span>dialog opens.</li><li>Go to<span> </span><strong>General</strong><span> </span>&gt;<span> </span><strong>UAF</strong>, and in the<span> </span><strong>Default Constraint Owners</strong><span> </span>property group, choose a needed owner.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="454d05a9-9e7f-4f58-8b1b-b027f18eaa7d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">414963390</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Constraint" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Performer" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Activity" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Information" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with OV-6a table" ri:space-key="UAF12P2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=191926806 space=UAF12P2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00678: MODAF. OV-6b Operational State Transition Description

- page_id: `191926806`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024xR2/pages/191926806/MODAF.+OV-6b+Operational+State+Transition+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-14T07:52:36.520+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

415053735

415053738

415053736

**Description**

The Operational State Transition Description is a graphical method of describing how an operational node or activity responds to various events by changing its state. The diagram represents the sets of events to which the architecture will respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action. The explicit sequencing of activities in response to external and internal events is not fully expressed in an OV-5. An OV-6b can be used to describe the explicit sequencing of the operational activities.

Alternatively, an OV-6b can be used to reflect the explicit sequencing of actions internal to a single operational activity or the sequencing of operational activities with respect to a specific operational node. In a net-centric architecture, the OV-6b is used to describe the set of state transitions for providers and consumers in the NetCentric Environment (NCE) in response to the posting of information to the NCE or retrieving of information from the NCE.

**Implementation**

OV-6b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### OV-6b Operational State Transition Description

**Related views**

An OV-6b can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. The OV-6b is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in the Activity Model (OV-5).

415053734

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational State Description' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational States' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Performer' space='UAF12P2024xR2']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='UAF12P2024xR2']
